self.stdout.write(\n    72→                    self.style.WARNING(\n    73→                        f\"  SKIP  enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email}): \"\n    74→                        f\"AiCertsCourse id={enrollment.object_id} not found.\"\n    75→                    )\n    76→                )\n    77→                counts['skipped'] += 1\n    78→                continue\n    79→\n    80→            already_exists = AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(\n    81→                user=user, course=course\n    82→            ).exists()\n    83→\n    84→            if already_exists:\n    85→                existing = AICertsEnrollment.objects.get(user=user, course=course)\n    86→                self.stdout.write(\n    87→                    f\"  OK    enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email} → {course.title}): \"\n    88→                    f\"AICertsEnrollment already exists \"\n    89→                    f\"(status={existing.aicerts_enrollment_status}).\"\n    90→                )\n    91→                counts['skipped'] += 1\n    92→                continue\n    93→\n    94→            self.stdout.write(\n    95→                f\"  SYNC  enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email} → {course.title}) ...\"\n    96→            )\n    97→\n    98→            if dry_run:\n    99→                self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS(\"        [DRY RUN] would call EnrollmentSyncService\"))\n   100→                counts['synced'] += 1\n   101→                continue\n   102→\n   103→            try:\n   104→                EnrollmentSyncService.enroll_user_in_course(user=user, course=course)\n   105→                self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS(\"        SUCCESS\"))\n   106→                counts['synced'] += 1\n   107→            except Exception as e:\n   108→                self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR(f\"        FAILED: {e}\"))\n   109→                # Create a pending record so the daily retry task picks it up\n   110→                try:\n   111→                    AICertsEnrollment.objects.get_or_create(\n   112→                        user=user,\n   113→                        course=course,\n   114→                        defaults={\n   115→                            'aicerts_enrollment_status': 'pending',\n   116→                            'sync_error': str(e)[:500],\n   117→                        }\n   118→                    )\n   119→                    self.stdout.write(\n   120→                        self.style.WARNING(\n   121→                            \"        Created pending AICertsEnrollment — \"\n   122→                            \"will be retried by the daily Celery task.\"\n   123→                        )\n   124→                    )\n   125→                    counts['pending'] += 1\n   126→                except Exception as inner_e:\n   127→                    self.stdout.write(\n   128→                        self.style.ERROR(f\"        Could not create pending record: {inner_e}\")\n   129→                    )\n   130→                    counts['failed'] += 1\n   131→\n   132→        self.stdout.write(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n   133→        self.stdout.write(\n   134→            f\"Done.  \"\n   135→            f\"Synced: {counts['synced']}  |  \"\n   136→            f\"Already existed (skipped): {counts['skipped']}  |  \"\n   137→            f\"Pending (retry queued): {counts['pending']}  |  \"\n   138→            f\"Hard failed: {counts['failed']}\"\n   139→        )\n   140→        if dry_run:\n   141→            self.stdout.write(self.style.WARNING(\"(DRY RUN — no changes were made)\"))\n   142→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. apps/aicerts_integration/management/commands/backfill_aicerts_enrollments.py\n\"\"\"\nManagement command to backfill AICERTS enrollments for students who paid for\nan AICERTS course before the automatic sync was wired into FinalizeEnrollmentView.\n\nUsage:\n    python manage.py backfill_aicerts_enrollments\n    python manage.py backfill_aicerts_enrollments --dry-run\n    python manage.py backfill_aicerts_enrollments --email richard@example.com\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom django.core.management.base import BaseCommand\nfrom django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType\n\n\nclass Command(BaseCommand):\n    help = (\n        \"Backfill AICertsEnrollment records and AICERTS-side enrollment for \"\n        \"students who have a paid Enrollment for an AICERTS course but no \"\n        \"matching AICertsEnrollment record.\"\n    )\n\n    def add_arguments(self, parser):\n        parser.add_argument(\n            '--dry-run',\n            action='store_true',\n            help='Show what would be done without making any changes.',\n        )\n        parser.add_argument(\n            '--email',\n            type=str,\n            default=None,\n            help='Only process enrollments for this specific user email.',\n        )\n\n    def handle(self, *args, **options):\n        from apps.payments.models import Enrollment, EnrollmentStatus\n        from apps.aicerts_courses.models import AiCertsCourse\n        from apps.aicerts_integration.models import AICertsEnrollment\n        from apps.aicerts_integration.services import EnrollmentSyncService\n\n        dry_run = options['dry_run']\n        target_email = options['email']\n\n        if dry_run:\n            self.stdout.write(self.style.WARNING(\"DRY RUN — no changes will be made.\\n\"))\n\n        # Find all paid Enrollment rows that point at an AiCertsCourse\n        aicerts_ct = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(AiCertsCourse)\n        qs = Enrollment.objects.filter(\n            content_type=aicerts_ct,\n            status=EnrollmentStatus.ENROLLED,\n        ).select_related('user')\n\n        if target_email:\n            qs = qs.filter(user__email__iexact=target_email)\n\n        total = qs.count()\n        self.stdout.write(\n            f\"Found {total} paid AICERTS enrollment(s) to inspect\"\n            f\"{f' for {target_email}' if target_email else ''}.\\n\"\n        )\n\n        counts = {'skipped': 0, 'synced': 0, 'pending': 0, 'failed': 0}\n\n        for enrollment in qs:\n            user = enrollment.user\n            course = AiCertsCourse.objects.filter(id=enrollment.object_id).first()\n\n            if not course:\n                self.stdout.write(\n                    self.style.WARNING(\n                        f\"  SKIP  enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email}): \"\n                        f\"AiCertsCourse id={enrollment.object_id} not found.\"\n                    )\n                )\n                counts['skipped'] += 1\n                continue\n\n            already_exists = AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(\n                user=user, course=course\n            ).exists()\n\n            if already_exists:\n                existing = AICertsEnrollment.objects.get(user=user, course=course)\n                self.stdout.write(\n                    f\"  OK    enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email} → {course.title}): \"\n                    f\"AICertsEnrollment already exists \"\n                    f\"(status={existing.aicerts_enrollment_status}).\"\n                )\n                counts['skipped'] += 1\n                continue\n\n            self.stdout.write(\n                f\"  SYNC  enrollment {enrollment.id} ({user.email} → {course.title}) ...\"\n            )\n\n            if dry_run:\n                self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS(\"        [DRY RUN] would call EnrollmentSyncService\"))\n                counts['synced'] += 1\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                EnrollmentSyncService.enroll_user_in_course(user=user, course=course)\n                self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS(\"        SUCCESS\"))\n                counts['synced'] += 1\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR(f\"        FAILED: {e}\"))\n                # Create a pending record so the daily retry task picks it up\n                try:\n                    AICertsEnrollment.objects.get_or_create(\n                        user=user,\n                        course=course,\n                        defaults={\n                            'aicerts_enrollment_status': 'pending',\n                            'sync_error': str(e)[:500],\n                        }\n                    )\n                    self.stdout.write(\n                        self.style.WARNING(\n                            \"        Created pending AICertsEnrollment — \"\n                            \"will be retried by the daily Celery task.\"\n                        )\n                    )\n                    counts['pending'] += 1\n                except Exception as inner_e:\n                    self.stdout.write(\n                        self.style.ERROR(f\"        Could not create pending record: {inner_e}\")\n                    )\n                    counts['failed'] += 1\n\n        self.stdout.write(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n        self.stdout.write(\n            f\"Done.  \"\n            f\"Synced: {counts['synced']}  |  \"\n            f\"Already existed (skipped): {counts['skipped']}  |  \"\n            f\"Pending (retry queued): {counts['pending']}  |  \"\n            f\"Hard failed: {counts['failed']}\"\n        )\n        if dry_run:\n            self.stdout.write(self.style.WARNING(\"(DRY RUN — no changes were made)\"))\n","numLines":142,"startLine":1,"totalLines":142}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a5e78311-b17e-4689-83d9-f2c6f4103eb2"}

   CACHE_TTL = 3600  # Cache course data for 1 hour\n\n    @classmethod\n    def get_courses(cls, page: int = 1, per_page: int = 100, search: str = None) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch paginated list of courses from AICERTs.\n\n        Args:\n            page: Page number\n            per_page: Items per page (max 100)\n            search: Optional search keyword\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with 'success', 'data', 'total', 'total_pages'\n\n        Raises:\n            AICERTsAPIError: On API failure\n        \"\"\"\n        params = {\n            'page': page,\n            'per_page': min(per_page, 100)\n        }\n\n        if search:\n            params['search'] = search\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(\n                f\"{cls.BASE_URL}/courses\",\n                params=params,\n                timeout=settings.AICERTS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT\n            )\n            response.raise_for_status()\n\n            data = response.json()\n            if not data.get('success'):\n                raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course list API returned success=false\")\n\n            logger.info(f\"Fetched {len(data.get('data', []))} courses from AICERTs (page {page})\")\n            return data\n\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to fetch courses from AICERTs: {e}\")\n            raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course list API failed: {e}\")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def get_course_detail(cls, course_id: int, use_cache: bool = True) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch detailed information for a specific course.\n\n        Args:\n            course_id: AICERTs course ID\n            use_cache: Whether to use cached data\n\n        Returns:\n            Course detail dict\n\n        Raises:\n            AICERTsAPIError: On API failure or course not found\n        \"\"\"\n        cache_key = f\"aicerts_course_{course_id}\"\n\n        if use_cache:\n            cached_data = cache.get(cache_key)\n            if cached_data:\n                logger.debug(f\"Returning cached data for course {course_id}\")\n                return cached_data\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(\n                f\"{cls.BASE_URL}/course/{course_id}\",\n                timeout=settings.AICERTS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT\n            )\n            response.raise_for_status()\n\n            data = response.json()\n            if not data.get('success'):\n                raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course detail API returned success=false for ID {course_id}\")\n\n            course_data = data.get('data')\n            if not course_data:\n                raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course {course_id} not found\")\n\n            # Cache the result\n            cache.set(cache_key, course_data, cls.CACHE_TTL)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Fetched details for course {course_id}: {course_data.get('title')}\")\n            return course_data\n\n        except requests.HTTPError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course {course_id} not found\")\n            logger.error(f\"HTTP error fetching course {course_id}: {e}\")\n            raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course detail API failed: {e}\")\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to fetch course {course_id} from AICERTs: {e}\")\n            raise AICERTsAPIError(f\"Course detail API failed: {e}\")\n\n    @classmethod\n    def sync_all_courses(cls, callback=None) -> Tuple[int, int, List[str]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Sync all courses from AICERTs Course Data API.\n\n        Args:\n            callback: Optional function called for each course: callback(course_data)\n\n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (total_courses, new_courses, errors)\n        \"\"\"\n        total_courses = 0\n        new_courses = 0\n        errors = []\n        page = 1\n\n        logger.info(\"Starting full course sync from AICERTs...\")\n\n        while True:\n            try:\n                result = cls.get_courses(page=page, per_page=100)\n                courses = result.get('data', [])\n\n                if not courses:\n                    break\n\n                for course in courses:\n                    try:\n                        # Fetch full details\n                        course_id = course.get('id')\n                        if not course_id:\n                            continue\n\n                        detail = cls.get_course_detail(course_id, use_cache=False)\n\n                        # Call callback if provided\n                        if callback:\n                            is_new = callback(detail)\n                            if is_new:\n                                new_courses += 1\n\n                        total_courses += 1\n\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        error_msg = f\"Error syncing course {course.get('id')}: {e}\"\n                        logger.error(error_msg)\n                        errors.append(error_msg)\n\n                # Check if there are more pages\n                if page >= result.get('total_pages', 1):\n                    break\n\n                page += 1\n\n            except AICERTsAPIError as e:\n                error_msg = f\"Error fetching page {page}: {e}\"\n                logger.error(error_msg)\n                errors.append(error_msg)\n                break\n\n        logger.info(f\"Course sync complete: {total_courses} total, {new_courses} new, {len(errors)} errors\")\n        return total_courses, new_courses, errors\n\n\nclass SSOService:\n    \"\"\"\n    Service for AICERTs SSO API interactions.\n    Base URL: https://learn.aicerts.io/webservice/rest/server.php\n\n    Handles:\n    - User creation\n    - Course enrollment\n    - Authentication/auto-login\n    \"\"\"\n\n    BASE_URL = settings.AICERTS_SSO_BASE_URL\n    WSTOKEN = settings.AICERTS_WSTOKEN\n    PARTNER_ID = settings.AICERTS_PARTNER_ID\n    REST_FORMAT = settings.AICERTS_REST_FORMAT\n\n    @classmethod\n    def _build_base_params(cls, wsfunction: str) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"Build base parameters for all SSO API calls\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'wstoken': cls.WSTOKEN,\n            'wsfunction': wsfunction,\n            'moodlewsrestformat': cls.REST_FORMAT\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def create_user(cls, email: str, first_name: str, last_name: str, username: str = None) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new user in AICERTs LMS.\n\n        Args:\n            email: User email\n            first_name: User's first name\n            last_name: User's last name\n {"parentUuid":"cf0bca00-a9fc-40cd-96e7-bd417a7d6182","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01647yNbZy74XYRQw4BNWeNF","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01WJFZUNzESxkeUwx96Qo6bF","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"docker exec lms-prod-backend-1 python manage.py shell -c \"\nfrom django.contrib.auth import get_user_model\nfrom django.utils import timezone\n\nUser = get_user_model()\n\n# Create Richard Masukume's account\nemail = 'richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za'\npassword = 'password123'\n\nif User.objects.filter(email=email).exists():\n    u = User.objects.get(email=email)\n    u.set_password(password)\n    u.is_active = True\n    u.confirmed = True\n    u.suspended = False\n    u.save()\n    print(f'Updated existing user: {email}')\nelse:\n    u = User.objects.create_user(\n        username='richard.masukume',\n        email=email,\n        password=password,\n        first_name='Richard',\n        last_name='Masukume',\n        name='Richard Masukume',\n        fullname='Richard Masukume',\n        role_id=3,  # {"parentUuid":"d7cbee12-7872-4aa0-a213-ddfb1040f50b","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01NPqqLRmoRpzyizaEfa5uAR","toolUseID":"toolu_01NPqqLRmoRpzyizaEfa5uAR","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:46:50.853Z","uuid":"f641fa54-3762-440d-9642-b3d70b1d1fb7"}
{"parentUuid":"d7cbee12-7872-4aa0-a213-ddfb1040f50b","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NPqqLRmoRpzyizaEfa5uAR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py\n     2→\"\"\"\n     3→Signals for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n     4→\n     5→Handles:\n     6→- Automatic user synchronization with AICERTs on registration\n     7→- Instructor validation for course assignments\n     8→- Enrollment tracking\n     9→\"\"\"\n    10→\n    11→from django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\n    12→from django.dispatch import receiver\n    13→from django.conf import settings\n    14→from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\n    15→from django.utils import timezone\n    16→import logging\n    17→\n    18→from apps.users.models import User\n    19→from .models import (\n    20→    AICertsEnrollment,\n    21→    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    22→    AICertsSyncLog\n    23→)\n    24→from .services import (\n    25→    SSOService,\n    26→    AICERTsAPIError\n    27→)\n    28→\n    29→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    30→\n    31→\n    32→@receiver(post_save, sender=User)\n    33→def auto_create_aicerts_user(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    34→    \"\"\"\n    35→    Automatically create user on AICERTs when they register on Hosi Academy.\n    36→    Only runs if AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS is enabled and user is email verified.\n    37→    \"\"\"\n    38→    if not created:\n    39→        # Only run for new users\n    40→        return\n    41→\n    42→    if not getattr(settings, 'AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS', False):\n    43→        logger.debug(\"Auto-create AICERTs users disabled in settings\")\n    44→        return\n    45→\n    46→    if not instance.email:\n    47→        logger.warning(f\"User {instance.id} has no email, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n    48→        return\n    49→\n    50→    # Skip if user already has AICERTs ID\n    51→    if instance.aicerts_user_id:\n    52→        logger.debug(f\"User {instance.email} already has AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id}\")\n    53→        return\n    54→\n    55→    # Check if email is verified (optional - adjust based on requirements)\n    56→    if not instance.email_verified_at:\n    57→        logger.info(f\"User {instance.email} email not verified, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n    58→        return\n    59→\n    60→    try:\n    61→        # Create user on AICERTs\n    62→        result = SSOService.create_user(\n    63→            email=instance.email,\n    64→            first_name=instance.first_name or instance.username,\n    65→            last_name=instance.last_name or '',\n    66→            username=instance.email\n    67→        )\n    68→\n    69→        aicerts_user_id = result.get('id')\n    70→\n    71→        # Update local user record\n    72→        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n    73→            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n    74→            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()\n    75→        )\n    76→\n    77→        # Log successful sync\n    78→        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n    79→            operation_type='user_create',\n    80→            status='success',\n    81→            user=instance,\n    82→            response_data=result\n    83→        )\n    84→\n    85→        logger.info(f\"Created AICERTs user for {instance.email} (ID: {aicerts_user_id})\")\n    86→\n    87→    except AICERTsAPIError as e:\n    88→        logger.error(f\"Failed to create AICERTs user for {instance.email}: {e}\")\n    89→\n    90→        # Log failed sync\n    91→        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n    92→            operation_type='user_create',\n    93→            status='failed',\n    94→            user=instance,\n    95→            error_message=str(e)\n    96→        )\n    97→\n    98→\n    99→@receiver(m2m_changed, sender=User.aicerts_instructor_courses.through)\n   100→def validate_instructor_designation(sender, instance, action, pk_set, **kwargs):\n   101→    \"\"\"\n   102→    Validate that only registered AICERTs instructors can be assigned courses.\n   103→    Enforces partnership rule: must be is_aicerts_instructor=True.\n   104→    \"\"\"\n   105→    if action != \"pre_add\":\n   106→        return\n   107→\n   108→    if not instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n   109→        raise ValidationError(\n   110→            f\"User {instance.get_full_name()} ({instance.email}) is not a registered \"\n   111→            f\"AICERTs instructor. Please set is_aicerts_instructor=True before \"\n   112→            f\"assigning courses.\"\n   113→        )\n   114→\n   115→    # Optionally: Check if instructor has AICERTs account\n   116→    if not instance.aicerts_user_id:\n   117→        logger.warning(\n   118→            f\"Instructor {instance.email} is marked as AICERTs instructor but has no \"\n   119→            f\"AICERTs user ID. They may need to be created on AICERTs first.\"\n   120→        )\n   121→\n   122→\n   123→@receiver(post_save, sender=AICertsEnrollment)\n   124→def log_enrollment_changes(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n   125→    \"\"\"\n   126→    Log enrollment status changes for audit trail.\n   127→    \"\"\"\n   128→    if created:\n   129→        logger.info(\n   130→            f\"New enrollment created: {instance.user.email} → {instance.course.title} \"\n   131→            f\"(status: {instance.aicerts_enrollment_status})\"\n   132→        )\n   133→\n   134→\n   135→@receiver(pre_save, sender=User)\n   136→def validate_aicerts_instructor_flag(sender, instance, **kwargs):\n   137→    \"\"\"\n   138→    Log when users are marked as AICERTs instructors.\n   139→    Helps admins track instructor registrations.\n   140→    \"\"\"\n   141→    if instance.pk:  # Only for existing users\n   142→        try:\n   143→            old_instance = User.objects.get(pk=instance.pk)\n   144→            if not old_instance.is_aicerts_instructor and instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n   145→                logger.info(\n   146→                    f\"User {instance.email} marked as AICERTs instructor \"\n   147→                    f\"(AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id})\"\n   148→                )\n   149→        except User.DoesNotExist:\n   150→            pass\n   151→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"uuid":"4eec1a69-b3b5-4269-9a96-6b5ee0d41956","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:46:50.852Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py","content":"# apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py\n\"\"\"\nSignals for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n\nHandles:\n- Automatic user synchronization with AICERTs on registration\n- Instructor validation for course assignments\n- Enrollment tracking\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\nfrom django.dispatch import receiver\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nimport logging\n\nfrom apps.users.models import User\nfrom .models import (\n    AICertsEnrollment,\n    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    AICertsSyncLog\n)\nfrom .services import (\n    SSOService,\n    AICERTsAPIError\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=User)\ndef auto_create_aicerts_user(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Automatically create user on AICERTs when they register on Hosi Academy.\n    Only runs if AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS is enabled and user is email verified.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not created:\n        # Only run for new users\n        return\n\n    if not getattr(settings, 'AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS', False):\n        logger.debug(\"Auto-create AICERTs users disabled in settings\")\n        return\n\n    if not instance.email:\n        logger.warning(f\"User {instance.id} has no email, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    # Skip if user already has AICERTs ID\n    if instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.debug(f\"User {instance.email} already has AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id}\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if email is verified (optional - adjust based on requirements)\n    if not instance.email_verified_at:\n        logger.info(f\"User {instance.email} email not verified, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    try:\n        # Create user on AICERTs\n        result = SSOService.create_user(\n            email=instance.email,\n            first_name=instance.first_name or instance.username,\n            last_name=instance.last_name or '',\n            username=instance.email\n        )\n\n        aicerts_user_id = result.get('id')\n\n        # Update local user record\n        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()\n        )\n\n        # Log successful sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='success',\n            user=instance,\n            response_data=result\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created AICERTs user for {instance.email} (ID: {aicerts_user_id})\")\n\n    except AICERTsAPIError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to create AICERTs user for {instance.email}: {e}\")\n\n        # Log failed sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='failed',\n            user=instance,\n            error_message=str(e)\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(m2m_changed, sender=User.aicerts_instructor_courses.through)\ndef validate_instructor_designation(sender, instance, action, pk_set, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate that only registered AICERTs instructors can be assigned courses.\n    Enforces partnership rule: must be is_aicerts_instructor=True.\n    \"\"\"\n    if action != \"pre_add\":\n        return\n\n    if not instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n        raise ValidationError(\n            f\"User {instance.get_full_name()} ({instance.email}) is not a registered \"\n            f\"AICERTs instructor. Please set is_aicerts_instructor=True before \"\n            f\"assigning courses.\"\n        )\n\n    # Optionally: Check if instructor has AICERTs account\n    if not instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"Instructor {instance.email} is marked as AICERTs instructor but has no \"\n            f\"AICERTs user ID. They may need to be created on AICERTs first.\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=AICertsEnrollment)\ndef log_enrollment_changes(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log enrollment status changes for audit trail.\n    \"\"\"\n    if created:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"New enrollment created: {instance.user.email} → {instance.course.title} \"\n            f\"(status: {instance.aicerts_enrollment_status})\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(pre_save, sender=User)\ndef validate_aicerts_instructor_flag(sender, instance, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log when users are marked as AICERTs instructors.\n    Helps admins track instructor registrations.\n    \"\"\"\n    if instance.pk:  # Only for existing users\n        try:\n            old_instance = User.objects.get(pk=instance.pk)\n            if not old_instance.is_aicerts_instructor and instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"User {instance.email} marked as AICERTs instructor \"\n                    f\"(AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id})\"\n                )\n        except User.DoesNotExist:\n            pass\n","numLines":151,"startLine":1,"totalLines":151}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d7cbee12-7872-4aa0-a213-ddfb1040f50b"}
{"parentUuid":"4eec1a69-b3b5-4269-9a96-6b5ee0d41956","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01Qyttr1XFHFGNoab9Vivb9Q","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The signal `auto_create_aicerts_user` tries to update `aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()`, but `aicerts_synced_at` is a `numeric(20,4)` field (epoch timestamp), not a datetime field. This is the bug causing the error.The signal is trying to set `aicerts_synced_at` to a datetime, but it needs to be an epoch timestamp (float/integer).\n\nHowever, before the signal updates the field, it calls `SSOService.create_user()` which makes an API call to AICERTS. If `AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS` is `True`, it will try to create the user in AICERTS and then update with the AICERTS user ID.\n\n\nThe signal fires when a new user is created with `email_verified_at` set, and if auto-creation is enabled, it calls the AICERTS service before attempting to update the timestamp field. The fix is to convert the datetime to an epoch timestamp using `time.time()` instead of passing the datetime object directly.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2138,"cache_read_input_tokens":68491,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2138,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"}},"requestId":"req_011CYc9XjPACLRdcE3MUPegw","type":"assistant","uuid":"eae49543-ac8d-477f-9e29-9fad67fa6ba5","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:47:00.775Z"}

successfully."}]},"uuid":"2db0f189-46e0-4290-bbe0-ecf2e759d9a7","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:47:56.452Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py","oldString":"from django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\nfrom django.dispatch import receiver\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nimport logging","newString":"from django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\nfrom django.dispatch import receiver\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nimport logging\nimport time","originalFile":"# apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py\n\"\"\"\nSignals for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n\nHandles:\n- Automatic user synchronization with AICERTs on registration\n- Instructor validation for course assignments\n- Enrollment tracking\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\nfrom django.dispatch import receiver\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nimport logging\n\nfrom apps.users.models import User\nfrom .models import (\n    AICertsEnrollment,\n    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    AICertsSyncLog\n)\nfrom .services import (\n    SSOService,\n    AICERTsAPIError\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=User)\ndef auto_create_aicerts_user(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Automatically create user on AICERTs when they register on Hosi Academy.\n    Only runs if AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS is enabled and user is email verified.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not created:\n        # Only run for new users\n        return\n\n    if not getattr(settings, 'AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS', False):\n        logger.debug(\"Auto-create AICERTs users disabled in settings\")\n        return\n\n    if not instance.email:\n        logger.warning(f\"User {instance.id} has no email, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    # Skip if user already has AICERTs ID\n    if instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.debug(f\"User {instance.email} already has AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id}\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if email is verified (optional - adjust based on requirements)\n    if not instance.email_verified_at:\n        logger.info(f\"User {instance.email} email not verified, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    try:\n        # Create user on AICERTs\n        result = SSOService.create_user(\n            email=instance.email,\n            first_name=instance.first_name or instance.username,\n            last_name=instance.last_name or '',\n            username=instance.email\n        )\n\n        aicerts_user_id = result.get('id')\n\n        # Update local user record\n        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()\n        )\n\n        # Log successful sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='success',\n            user=instance,\n            response_data=result\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created AICERTs user for {instance.email} (ID: {aicerts_user_id})\")\n\n    except AICERTsAPIError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to create AICERTs user for {instance.email}: {e}\")\n\n        # Log failed sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='failed',\n            user=instance,\n            error_message=str(e)\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(m2m_changed, sender=User.aicerts_instructor_courses.through)\ndef validate_instructor_designation(sender, instance, action, pk_set, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate that only registered AICERTs instructors can be assigned courses.\n    Enforces partnership rule: must be is_aicerts_instructor=True.\n    \"\"\"\n    if action != \"pre_add\":\n        return\n\n    if not instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n        raise ValidationError(\n            f\"User {instance.get_full_name()} ({instance.email}) is not a registered \"\n            f\"AICERTs instructor. Please set is_aicerts_instructor=True before \"\n            f\"assigning courses.\"\n        )\n\n    # Optionally: Check if instructor has AICERTs account\n    if not instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"Instructor {instance.email} is marked as AICERTs instructor but has no \"\n            f\"AICERTs user ID. They may need to be created on AICERTs first.\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=AICertsEnrollment)\ndef log_enrollment_changes(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log enrollment status changes for audit trail.\n    \"\"\"\n    if created:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"New enrollment created: {instance.user.email} → {instance.course.title} \"\n            f\"(status: {instance.aicerts_enrollment_status})\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(pre_save, sender=User)\ndef validate_aicerts_instructor_flag(sender, instance, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log when users are marked as AICERTs instructors.\n    Helps admins track instructor registrations.\n    \"\"\"\n    if instance.pk:  # Only for existing users\n        try:\n            old_instance = User.objects.get(pk=instance.pk)\n            if not old_instance.is_aicerts_instructor and instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"User {instance.email} marked as AICERTs instructor \"\n                    f\"(AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id})\"\n                )\n        except User.DoesNotExist:\n            pass\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":14,"oldLines":6,"newStart":14,"newLines":7,"lines":[" from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError"," from django.utils import timezone"," import logging","+import time"," "," from apps.users.models import User"," from .models import ("]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3eb93e9f-8081-4583-b48c-f17d07d775a6"}

Update local user record\n        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()\n        )","newString":"        # Update local user record (aicerts_synced_at is a numeric epoch timestamp)\n        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n            aicerts_synced_at=time.time()\n        )","originalFile":"# apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py\n\"\"\"\nSignals for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n\nHandles:\n- Automatic user synchronization with AICERTs on registration\n- Instructor validation for course assignments\n- Enrollment tracking\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom django.db.models.signals import post_save, pre_save, m2m_changed\nfrom django.dispatch import receiver\nfrom django.conf import settings\nfrom django.core.exceptions import ValidationError\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nimport logging\nimport time\n\nfrom apps.users.models import User\nfrom .models import (\n    AICertsEnrollment,\n    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    AICertsSyncLog\n)\nfrom .services import (\n    SSOService,\n    AICERTsAPIError\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=User)\ndef auto_create_aicerts_user(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Automatically create user on AICERTs when they register on Hosi Academy.\n    Only runs if AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS is enabled and user is email verified.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not created:\n        # Only run for new users\n        return\n\n    if not getattr(settings, 'AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS', False):\n        logger.debug(\"Auto-create AICERTs users disabled in settings\")\n        return\n\n    if not instance.email:\n        logger.warning(f\"User {instance.id} has no email, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    # Skip if user already has AICERTs ID\n    if instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.debug(f\"User {instance.email} already has AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id}\")\n        return\n\n    # Check if email is verified (optional - adjust based on requirements)\n    if not instance.email_verified_at:\n        logger.info(f\"User {instance.email} email not verified, skipping AICERTs creation\")\n        return\n\n    try:\n        # Create user on AICERTs\n        result = SSOService.create_user(\n            email=instance.email,\n            first_name=instance.first_name or instance.username,\n            last_name=instance.last_name or '',\n            username=instance.email\n        )\n\n        aicerts_user_id = result.get('id')\n\n        # Update local user record\n        User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n            aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()\n        )\n\n        # Log successful sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='success',\n            user=instance,\n            response_data=result\n        )\n\n        logger.info(f\"Created AICERTs user for {instance.email} (ID: {aicerts_user_id})\")\n\n    except AICERTsAPIError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to create AICERTs user for {instance.email}: {e}\")\n\n        # Log failed sync\n        AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n            operation_type='user_create',\n            status='failed',\n            user=instance,\n            error_message=str(e)\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(m2m_changed, sender=User.aicerts_instructor_courses.through)\ndef validate_instructor_designation(sender, instance, action, pk_set, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate that only registered AICERTs instructors can be assigned courses.\n    Enforces partnership rule: must be is_aicerts_instructor=True.\n    \"\"\"\n    if action != \"pre_add\":\n        return\n\n    if not instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n        raise ValidationError(\n            f\"User {instance.get_full_name()} ({instance.email}) is not a registered \"\n            f\"AICERTs instructor. Please set is_aicerts_instructor=True before \"\n            f\"assigning courses.\"\n        )\n\n    # Optionally: Check if instructor has AICERTs account\n    if not instance.aicerts_user_id:\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"Instructor {instance.email} is marked as AICERTs instructor but has no \"\n            f\"AICERTs user ID. They may need to be created on AICERTs first.\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(post_save, sender=AICertsEnrollment)\ndef log_enrollment_changes(sender, instance, created, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log enrollment status changes for audit trail.\n    \"\"\"\n    if created:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"New enrollment created: {instance.user.email} → {instance.course.title} \"\n            f\"(status: {instance.aicerts_enrollment_status})\"\n        )\n\n\n@receiver(pre_save, sender=User)\ndef validate_aicerts_instructor_flag(sender, instance, **kwargs):\n    \"\"\"\n    Log when users are marked as AICERTs instructors.\n    Helps admins track instructor registrations.\n    \"\"\"\n    if instance.pk:  # Only for existing users\n        try:\n            old_instance = User.objects.get(pk=instance.pk)\n            if not old_instance.is_aicerts_instructor and instance.is_aicerts_instructor:\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"User {instance.email} marked as AICERTs instructor \"\n                    f\"(AICERTs ID: {instance.aicerts_user_id})\"\n                )\n        except User.DoesNotExist:\n            pass\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":69,"oldLines":10,"newStart":69,"newLines":10,"lines":[" ","         aicerts_user_id = result.get('id')"," ","-        # Update local user record","+        # Update local user record (aicerts_synced_at is a numeric epoch timestamp)","         User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(","             aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,","-            aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()","+            aicerts_synced_at=time.time()","         )"," ","         # Log successful sync"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"eab5dfa1-1d33-4853-b776-85b187c50ead"}

{"parentUuid":"cace46c9-b72c-4b35-b0fe-d259bc656b83","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_016Ra1mZ9iarGtRyrESJBYxZ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Okay, so:1. `USERNAME_FIELD` is `username`, not `email`\n2. The SimplerJWT `TokenObtainPairView` authenticates by `username` (not email)\n3. Richard's username is `richard.masukume`\n4. The frontend sends `{'username': trimmedEmail, 'password': trimmedPassword}` - but it's using the email as the username field\n\nThis means the JWT login will fail even if the user exists in the database, because:\n- Frontend sends `username: 'richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za'` (the email address)\n\n\n- Django tries to find a user with `username='richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za'`\n- Richard's username is `richard.masukume` (not the email)\n- Auth fails\n\nI have a few options here: I could update Richard's username to match his email address, create a custom email-based authentication backend, or modify the JWT token view to handle email authentication. The cleanest approach would be either changing his username to the email or implementing a proper email backend, though the frontend code is already set up to send the email as the username field.\n\nLooking at how users are created, the `ApiClient.createUser()` method defaults to using email as the username when no username is explicitly provided, but the test commands create users with custom usernames like `john_student` and `sam_instructor`. Since the frontend always sends the email in the username field during login, the most straightforward fix is to update Richard's username to match his email address for consistency.\n\nActually, the better approach would be to add an authentication backend that supports email-based login, since the frontend is already structured to send email as the username field.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":300,"cache_read_input_tokens":77874,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":300,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":9,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"}},"requestId":"req_011CYc9sX826a8noGxcNX1et","type":"assistant","uuid":"47b958c7-69d7-41cc-aacf-7b1e548dd590","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:51:31.777Z"}

{"parentUuid":"1a7fdf13-d499-4c7c-a2b5-6960a479faec","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_019hf74E4fDmaCheBDrBvatZ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"There's no custom `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS` set in the project settings (only the default `ModelBackend`). The `TokenObtainPairView` from SimplerJWT uses the default Django auth backend, which authenticates by `username` field (not email).\n\nThe frontend sends:\n```dart\n'username': trimmedEmail,  // this is the email address\n```\n\nBut Django's ModelBackend looks up by `username` field. Richard's username is `richard.masukume`, not `richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za`.\n\nTwo options:\n1. **Update Richard's username to his email** - Quick fix but needs to be done for every user\n\n\n2. **Add an email authentication backend** - The proper solution is to create a custom backend that authenticates by email instead of username, then register it in `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS` in settings. I could either subclass `TokenObtainPairSerializer` to accept email, or create a custom authentication backend and configure SimplerJWT to use it.\n\nLooking at how SimplerJWT works, the `TokenObtainPairSerializer` calls `authenticate()` with username and password credentials, so I need to intercept that flow either at the serializer level or by providing a custom backend that maps email to the user.\n\nI'm creating a custom authentication backend that tries to fetch the user by email first, falling back to username lookup if needed, then validates the password and active status.\n\nWriting the backend class...\n\nNow I'll add this to a new `backends.py` file in the users app and register it in settings, then circle back to Richard's enrollment once the login issue is resolved.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2514,"cache_read_input_tokens":78954,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2514,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"}},"requestId":"req_011CYc9u2DGSvXfpNpbyjrTs","type":"assistant","uuid":"5f047dad-0669-46a8-8ade-1173fc6b6d51","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:51:56.943Z"}

{"parentUuid":"adc5ef95-d307-4796-a1e5-1dafcc8abc61","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_016UHQKgcbCeEiwHeiwVcCje","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017VR7DSNKKhVszQUJjjGdnB","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/users/backends.py","content":"from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model\nfrom django.contrib.auth.backends import ModelBackend\n\n\nclass EmailOrUsernameBackend(ModelBackend):\n    \"\"\"\n    Authenticate with either email or username.\n    The frontend always sends the email address in the 'username' field,\n    so this backend first tries a lookup by email, then falls back to username.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def authenticate(self, request, username=None, password=None, **kwargs):\n        User = get_user_model()\n\n        if username is None:\n            return None\n\n        # Try email lookup first\n        try:\n            user = User.objects.get(email__iexact=username)\n        except User.DoesNotExist:\n            # Fall back to username lookup\n            try:\n                user = User.objects.get(username__iexact=username)\n            except User.DoesNotExist:\n                return None\n\n        if user.check_password(password) and self.user_can_authenticate(user):\n            return user\n\n        return None\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1155,"cache_read_input_tokens":81468,"output_tokens":334,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1155},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CYc9vQsZZx2pbYZnKNjoa","type":"assistant","uuid":"00db3f78-49c8-473d-b788-96a38a76dfa8","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:52:04.100Z"}
{"parentUuid":"00db3f78-49c8-473d-b788-96a38a76dfa8","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Write","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_017VR7DSNKKhVszQUJjjGdnB","toolUseID":"toolu_017VR7DSNKKhVszQUJjjGdnB","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:52:05.124Z","uuid":"6685a54c-2b0c-489b-8d16-ee0163838b91"}
{"parentUuid":"00db3f78-49c8-473d-b788-96a38a76dfa8","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017VR7DSNKKhVszQUJjjGdnB","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/users/backends.py"}]},"uuid":"1224b6f3-e044-43cb-a9b1-37db39846236","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:52:05.121Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/users/backends.py","content":"from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model\nfrom django.contrib.auth.backends import ModelBackend\n\n\nclass EmailOrUsernameBackend(ModelBackend):\n    \"\"\"\n    Authenticate with either email or username.\n    The frontend always sends the email address in the 'username' field,\n    so this backend first tries a lookup by email, then falls back to username.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def authenticate(self, request, username=None, password=None, **kwargs):\n        User = get_user_model()\n\n        if username is None:\n            return None\n\n        # Try email lookup first\n        try:\n            user = User.objects.get(email__iexact=username)\n        except User.DoesNotExist:\n            # Fall back to username lookup\n            try:\n                user = User.objects.get(username__iexact=username)\n            except User.DoesNotExist:\n                return None\n\n        if user.check_password(password) and self.user_can_authenticate(user):\n            return user\n\n        return None\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"00db3f78-49c8-473d-b788-96a38a76dfa8"}

courses synced automatically.\n     6→Facilitators & Trainers Analytics and Assignment System.\n     7→\"\"\"\n     8→import os\n     9→import logging\n    10→from datetime import timedelta\n    11→from pathlib import Path\n    12→from decouple import config, Csv\n    13→\n    14→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    15→\n    16→# ==================== CELERY CONFIGURATION ====================\n    17→# Must be at the top for proper module loading\n    18→# from celery import Celery  # Temporarily disabled due to circular import\n    19→\n    20→# Set the default Django settings module for the Celery app\n    21→os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'lms_project.settings')\n    22→\n    23→# ==================== BUILD PATHS ====================\n    24→BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n    25→\n    26→# ==================== SECURITY & BASIC SETTINGS ====================\n    27→SECRET_KEY = config('SECRET_KEY', default='django-insecure-change-me-in-production-please')\n    28→DEBUG = config('DEBUG', default=True, cast=bool)\n    29→ALLOWED_HOSTS = config('ALLOWED_HOSTS', default='localhost,127.0.0.1', cast=Csv())\n    30→\n    31→# ==================== URL CONFIGURATION ====================\n    32→SITE_URL = config('SITE_URL', default='http://127.0.0.1:8000')\n    33→\n    34→# ==================== INSTALLED APPS ====================\n    35→INSTALLED_APPS = [\n    36→    'django.contrib.admin',\n    37→    'django.contrib.auth',\n    38→    'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n    39→    'django.contrib.sessions',\n    40→    'django.contrib.messages',\n    41→    'django.contrib.staticfiles',\n    42→    \n    43→    'corsheaders',\n    44→    'rest_framework',\n    45→    'rest_framework_simplejwt',\n    46→    \n    47→    'apps.users',\n    48→    'core',\n    49→    'apps.aicerts_courses',\n    50→    'apps.courses',\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"uuid":"871f1aa3-07cd-44c0-8817-d6796edd955e","timestamp":"2026-03-01T09:52:08.688Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/lms_project/settings.py","content":"\"\"\"\nDjango settings for lms_project project.\nGenerated and customized for Hosi Academy LMS monorepo (Django + Flutter).\nAfrica-focused, culturally resonant, and impact-driven.\nPartner integration with AI Certs - courses synced automatically.\nFacilitators & Trainers Analytics and Assignment System.\n\"\"\"\nimport os\nimport logging\nfrom datetime import timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom decouple import config, Csv\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n# ==================== CELERY CONFIGURATION ====================\n# Must be at the top for proper module loading\n# from celery import Celery  # Temporarily disabled due to circular import\n\n# Set the default Django settings module for the Celery app\nos.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'lms_project.settings')\n\n# ==================== BUILD PATHS ====================\nBASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n\n# ==================== SECURITY & BASIC SETTINGS ====================\nSECRET_KEY = config('SECRET_KEY', default='django-insecure-change-me-in-production-please')\nDEBUG = config('DEBUG', default=True, cast=bool)\nALLOWED_HOSTS = config('ALLOWED_HOSTS', default='localhost,127.0.0.1', cast=Csv())\n\n# ==================== URL CONFIGURATION ====================\nSITE_URL = config('SITE_URL', default='http://127.0.0.1:8000')\n\n# ==================== INSTALLED APPS ====================\nINSTALLED_APPS = [\n    'django.contrib.admin',\n    'django.contrib.auth',\n    'django.contrib.contenttypes',\n    'django.contrib.sessions',\n    'django.contrib.messages',\n    'django.contrib.staticfiles',\n    \n    'corsheaders',\n    'rest_framework',\n    'rest_framework_simplejwt',\n    \n    'apps.users',\n    'core',\n    'apps.aicerts_courses',\n    'apps.courses',","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":668}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"6b3fe1e3-d5d3-47c1-ac90-1269f93b49d4"}

'django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend',\n]","originalFile":"﻿\"\"\"\nDjango settings for lms_project project.\nGenerated and customized for Hosi Academy LMS monorepo (Django + Flutter).\nAfrica-focused, culturally resonant, and impact-driven.\nPartner integration with AI Certs - courses synced automatically.\nFacilitators & Trainers Analytics and Assignment System.\n\"\"\"\nimport os\nimport logging\nfrom datetime import timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom decouple import config, Csv\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n# ==================== 'propagate': False,\n        },\n    },\n}\n\n# AICerts Global Settings (Flat structure used by integration services)\nAICERTS_COURSE_API_BASE_URL = config('AICERTS_COURSE_API_BASE_URL', mapping\nINDUSTRY_MAPPING = {\n    'Healthcare': ['healthcare', 'health', 'doctor', 'medical', 'pharma', 'nurse'],\n    'Mining': ['mining'],\n    'Real Estate': ['real estate'],\n 'Sustainability': ['sustainability', 'environment', 'green'],\n    'AI Agent': ['agent', 'automation', 'bot'],\n    'Data & Robotics': ['data', 'robotics', 'machine learning', 'ai'],\n    'Cloud ai'],\n    'Project Management': ['project management', 'project manager', 'program director', 'scrum'],\n    'Product Management': ['product manager', 'product owner'],\n    'Education': ['educator', 'Security': ['security', 'hacker', 'network', 'compliance', 'cybersecurity'],\n    'Audio': ['audio', 'sound', 'music'],\n    'General AI': [],\n}\n\n# ==================== EMAIL CONFIGURATION ====================\nEMAIL_BACKEND = config('EMAIL_BACKEND', default='django.core.mail.backends.console.EmailBackend')\nEMAIL_HOST = config('EMAIL_HOST', default='smtp.gmail.com')\nEMAIL_PORT = config('EMAIL_PORT', default=587, cast=int)\nEMAIL_USE_TLS = config('EMAIL_USE_TLS', default=True, cast=bool)\nEMAIL_HOST_USER = config('EMAIL_HOST_USER', default='')\nEMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = config('EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD', default='')\nDEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL = config('DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL', default='Hosi Academy <noreply@hosiacademy.com>')\n\n# ==================== TWILIO SMS CONFIGURATION ====================\n# For payment success SMS notifications\nTWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID = config('TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID', default='')\nTWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN = config('TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN', default='')\nTWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER = config('TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER', default='')\nTWILIO_WHATSAPP_NUMBER = config('TWILIO_WHATSAPP_NUMBER', default='')\n\n# ==================== SENTRY ERROR TRACKING ====================\n# Comprehensive error monitoring and performance tracking\nSENTRY_DSN = config('SENTRY_DSN', default='')\nSENTRY_ENABLED = config('SENTRY_ENABLED', default=not DEBUG, cast=bool)\n\nif SENTRY_DSN and SENTRY_ENABLED:\n    import sentry_sdk\n    from sentry_sdk.integrations.django import DjangoIntegration\n    from sentry_sdk.integrations.celery import CeleryIntegration\n    from sentry_sdk.integrations.redis import RedisIntegration\n    from sentry_sdk.integrations.logging import LoggingIntegration\n\n    # Configure logging integration\n    sentry_logging = LoggingIntegration(\n        level=logging.INFO,        # Capture info and above as breadcrumbs\n        event_level=logging.ERROR  # Send errors as events\n    )\n\n    def before_send(event, hint):\n        \"\"\"\n        Filter and modify events before sending to Sentry\n        Can be used to scrub sensitive data, filter spam, etc.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Don't send events in development unless explicitly enabled\n        if DEBUG and not config('SENTRY_DEBUG_MODE', default=False, cast=bool):\n            return None\n\n        # Scrub sensitive data from event\n        if 'request' in event:\n            # Remove sensitive headers\n            headers = event['request'].get('headers', {})\n            sensitive_headers = ['Authorization', 'Cookie', 'X-API-Key']\n            for header in sensitive_headers:\n                if header in headers:\n                    headers[header] = '[Filtered]'\n\n        # Filter out specific errors (if needed)\n        if 'exception' in event:\n            exception_type = event['exception']['values'][0]['type']\n            # Example: Ignore DisallowedHost errors in production\n            if exception_type == 'DisallowedHost':\n                return None\n\n        return event\n\n    def traces_sampler(sampling_context):\n        \"\"\"\n        Dynamically sample traces based on transaction type\n        Payment transactions: 100% sampling\n        Regular requests: 10% sampling\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if this is a payment transaction\n        if sampling_context.get('parent_sampled') is not None:\n            return sampling_context['parent_sampled']\n\n        # Get transaction name\n        transaction_name = sampling_context.get('transaction_context', {}).get('name', '')\n\n        # Sample 100% of payment-related transactions\n        if 'payment' in transaction_name.lower():\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Sample 100% of webhook transactions\n        if 'webhook' in transaction_name.lower():\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Sample 50% of API endpoints\n        if '/api/' in transaction_name:\n            return 0.5\n\n        # Sample 10% of everything else\n        return config('SENTRY_TRACES_SAMPLE_RATE', default=0.1, cast=float)\n\n    sentry_sdk.init(\n        dsn=SENTRY_DSN,\n\n        # Integrations\n        integrations=[\n            DjangoIntegration(\n                transaction_style='url',  # Use URL pattern as transaction name\n                middleware_spans=True,     # Create spans for middleware\n                signals_spans=True,        # Create spans for signals\n                cache_spans=True,          # Monitor cache operations\n            ),\n            CeleryIntegration(\n                monitor_beat_tasks=True,   # Monitor Celery Beat scheduled tasks\n                propagate_traces=True,     # Propagate traces from sync to async\n            ),\n            RedisIntegration(),\n            sentry_logging,\n        ],\n\n        # Performance Monitoring\n        traces_sampler=traces_sampler,      # Dynamic sampling\n        enable_tracing=True,                # Enable performance monitoring\n\n        # Profiling (captures function-level performance data)\n        profiles_sample_rate=config('SENTRY_PROFILES_SAMPLE_RATE', default=0.1, cast=float),\n\n        # Environment Configuration\n        environment=ENVIRONMENT,            # development, staging, production\n        release=config('SENTRY_RELEASE', default=None),  # Git commit SHA\n\n        # Data Scrubbing\n        send_default_pii=False,             # Don't send PII by default\n        before_send=before_send,            # Custom event filtering\n\n        # Error Handling\n        attach_stacktrace=True,             # Attach stacktraces to messages\n        max_breadcrumbs=50,                 # Keep last 50 breadcrumbs\n        debug=config('SENTRY_DEBUG', default=False, cast=bool),\n\n        # Request Data\n        request_bodies='medium',            # Capture medium-sized request bodies\n        max_request_body_size='medium',     # Medium: 10KB\n\n        # Ignored Errors (add patterns you want to ignore)\n        ignore_errors=[\n            # Ignore common browser errors\n            'ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded',\n            'ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications',\n        ],\n\n        # Tags (for filtering in Sentry UI)\n        _experiments={\n            'profiles_sample_rate': config('SENTRY_PROFILES_SAMPLE_RATE', default=0.1, cast=float),\n        }\n    )\n\n    # Log Sentry initialization\n    logger.info(\n        \"Sentry initialized\",\n        extra={\n            'environment': ENVIRONMENT,\n            'traces_sample_rate': 'dynamic',\n            'profiles_sample_rate': config('SENTRY_PROFILES_SAMPLE_RATE', default=0.1, cast=float),\n        }\n    )\n\nelif DEBUG:\n    logger.info(\"Sentry disabled in DEBUG mode\")\nelse:\n    logger.warning(\"Sentry DSN not configured - error tracking disabled\")\n\n# ==================== SECURITY (PRODUCTION) ====================\nif not DEBUG:\n    SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO', 'https')\n    SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER = True\n    SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF = True\n    X_FRAME_OPTIONS = 'DENY'\n    SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True\n    CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True\n    SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS = 31536000\n    SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS = True\n    SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD = True\n    SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True\nelse:\n    CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS = True\n    CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS = True\n\n# ==================== FILE UPLOAD & MEDIA ====================\nFILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE = 10485760  # 10MB\nDATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE = 10485760\n\nFACILITATOR_MEDIA_ROOT = MEDIA_ROOT / 'facilitators'\nFACILITATOR_MEDIA_URL = MEDIA_URL + 'facilitators/'\n\n# Create logs directory\nlog_dir = BASE_DIR / 'logs'\nif not os.path.exists(log_dir):\n    os.makedirs(log_dir)\n\n# ==================== ENVIRONMENT INFO ====================\nENVIRONMENT = config('ENVIRONMENT', default='development')\n\nif DEBUG:\n    print(f\"\\n{'='*60}\")\n    print(f\"Environment: {ENVIRONMENT}\")\n    print(f\"Debug: {DEBUG}\")\n    print(f\"Facilitators Module: ENABLED\")\n    print(f\"Auto Assignment: {FACILITATORS_CONFIG['AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_ENABLED']}\")\n    print(f\"AICerts Sync: {AICERTS_CONFIG['ENABLE_AUTO_SYNC']}\")\n    print(f\"Socket.io Enabled: {SOCKETIO_CONFIG['ENABLED']}\")\n    print(f\"Socket.io Port: {SOCKETIO_CONFIG['PORT']}\")\n
