    1→from rest_framework import serializers\n     2→from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType\n     3→from .models import (\n     4→    StudentProfile, Wishlist, CourseCart, CourseCartItem,\n     5→    CourseProvider, CourseCatalogItem\n     6→)\n     7→from apps.localization.models import Country, State, City\n     8→from apps.payments.serializer_fields import LocalizedPriceField, CurrencyField, FormattedPriceField\n     9→\n    10→\n    11→class LearnerProfileSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n    12→    \"\"\"Serializer for learner profile with previous payment details\"\"\"\n    13→\n    14→    user_email = serializers.EmailField(source='user.email', read_only=True)\n    15→    user_full_name = serializers.CharField(source='user.get_full_name', read_only=True)\n    16→\n    17→    preferred_country_name = serializers.CharField(source='preferred_country.name', read_only=True)\n    18→    preferred_state_name = serializers.CharField(source='preferred_state.name', read_only=True)\n    19→    preferred_city_name = serializers.CharField(source='preferred_city.name', read_only=True)\n    20→\n    21→    company_details = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    22→\n    23→    class Meta:\n    24→        model = StudentProfile\n    25→        fields = [\n    26→            'id', 'user', 'user_email', 'user_full_name',\n    27→            'last_used_company_name', 'last_used_company_email',\n    28→            'last_used_company_phone', 'last_used_company_address',\n    29→            'last_used_vat_number', 'has_company_payment_history',\n    30→            'preferred_country', 'preferred_country_name',\n    31→            'preferred_state', 'preferred_state_name',\n    32→            'preferred_city', 'preferred_city_name',\n    33→            'company_details', 'created_at', 'updated_at'\n    34→        ]\n    35→        read_only_fields = ['user', 'has_company_payment_history', 'created_at', 'updated_at']\n    36→\n    37→    def get_company_details(self, obj):\n    38→        \"\"\"Get formatted company details for reuse prompt\"\"\"\n    39→        if obj.has_company_payment_history:\n    40→            return obj.get_company_details()\n    41→        return None\n    42→\n    43→\n    44→class WishlistSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n    45→    \"\"\"Serializer for wishlist items with course details\"\"\"\n    46→\n    47→    user_email = serializers.EmailField(source='user.email', read_only=True)\n    48→    course_title = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    49→    course_details = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    50→    days_in_wishlist = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    51→\n    52→    class Meta:\n    53→        model = Wishlist\n    54→        fields = [\n    55→            'id', 'user', 'user_email', 'content_type', 'object_id',\n    56→            'course_title', 'course_details', 'training_type',\n    57→            'interest_level', 'intended_start', 'notes',\n    58→            'marketing_contacted', 'marketing_contacted_at', 'marketing_notes',\n    59→            'contacted_by', 'converted_to_cart', 'converted_to_enrollment',\n    60→            'days_in_wishlist', 'created_at', 'updated_at'\n    61→        ]\n    62→        read_only_fields = [\n    63→            'user', 'marketing_contacted', 'marketing_contacted_at',\n    64→            'contacted_by', 'converted_to_cart', 'converted_to_enrollment',\n    65→            'created_at', 'updated_at'\n    66→        ]\n    67→\n    68→    def get_course_title(self, obj):\n    69→        \"\"\"Get the title from the generic course\"\"\"\n    70→        if obj.course:\n    71→            return getattr(obj.course, 'title', str(obj.course))\n    72→        return 'Unknown Course'\n    73→\n    74→    def get_course_details(self, obj):\n    75→        \"\"\"Get course details for display\"\"\"\n    76→        if obj.course:\n    77→            return {\n    78→                'id': obj.object_id,\n    79→                'type': obj.content_type.model,\n    80→                'title': getattr(obj.course, 'title', ''),\n    81→                'description': getattr(obj.course, 'description', ''),\n    82→                'price': str(getattr(obj.course, 'price', 0)),\n    83→                'duration': getattr(obj.course, 'duration', ''),\n    84→            }\n    85→        return None\n    86→\n    87→    def get_days_in_wishlist(self, obj):\n    88→        \"\"\"Calculate days since added to wishlist\"\"\"\n    89→        from django.utils import timezone\n    90→        delta = timezone.now() - obj.created_at\n    91→        return delta.days\n    92→\n    93→\n    94→class CourseCartItemSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n    95→    \"\"\"Serializer for individual cart items\"\"\"\n    96→\n    97→    course_title = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    98→    course_details = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n    99→    \n   100→    # Localized Pricing (Shadowing model field)\n   101→    price = LocalizedPriceField(read_only=True)\n   102→    currency = CurrencyField(read_only=True)\n   103→    formatted_price = FormattedPriceField(source='*', price_field='price', read_only=True)\n   104→\n   105→    class Meta:\n   106→        model = CourseCartItem\n   107→        fields = [\n   108→            'id', 'cart', 'content_type', 'object_id',\n   109→            'course_title', 'course_details', 'training_type',\n   110→            'price', 'currency', 'formatted_price',\n   111→            'prerequisites_met', 'added_from_wishlist',\n   112→            'created_at'\n   113→        ]\n   114→        read_only_fields = ['cart', 'price', 'created_at']\n   115→\n   116→    def get_course_title(self, obj):\n   117→        \"\"\"Get the title from the generic course\"\"\"\n   118→        if obj.course:\n   119→            return getattr(obj.course, 'title', str(obj.course))\n   120→        return 'Unknown Course'\n   121→\n   122→    def get_course_details(self, obj):\n   123→        \"\"\"Get course details for display\"\"\"\n   124→        if obj.course:\n   125→            return {\n   126→                'id': obj.object_id,\n   127→                'type': obj.content_type.model,\n   128→                'title': getattr(obj.course, 'title', ''),\n   129→                'description': getattr(obj.course, 'description', ''),\n   130→                'duration': getattr(obj.course, 'duration', ''),\n   131→                'prerequisites': getattr(obj.course, 'prerequisites', []),\n   132→            }\n   133→        return None\n   134→\n   135→\n   136→class CourseCartSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   137→    \"\"\"Serializer for course cart with items\"\"\"\n   138→\n   139→    items = CourseCartItemSerializer(many=True, read_only=True)\n   140→    user_email = serializers.EmailField(source='user.email', read_only=True)\n   141→    previous_company_details = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   142→    \n   143→    # Localized Pricing (Shadowing model field)\n   144→    total_amount = LocalizedPriceField(read_only=True)\n   145→    currency = CurrencyField(read_only=True)\n   146→    formatted_total = FormattedPriceField(source='*', price_field='total_amount', read_only=True)\n   147→\n   148→    class Meta:\n   149→        model = CourseCart\n   150→        fields = [\n   151→            'id', 'user', 'user_email', 'status',\n   152→            'use_previous_company_details', 'is_corporate_enrollment',\n   153→            'total_courses', 'total_amount', 'currency', 'formatted_total',\n   154→            'items', 'previous_company_details', 'created_at', 'updated_at'\n   155→        ]\n   156→        read_only_fields = ['user', 'total_courses', 'total_amount', 'created_at', 'updated_at']\n   157→\n   158→    def get_previous_company_details(self, obj):\n   159→        \"\"\"Get previous company details if available\"\"\"\n   160→        try:\n   161→            profile = StudentProfile.objects.get(user=obj.user)\n   162→            if profile.has_company_payment_history:\n   163→                return profile.get_company_details()\n   164→        except StudentProfile.DoesNotExist:\n   165→            pass\n   166→        return None\n   167→\n   168→\n   169→class AddToCartSerializer(serializers.Serializer):\n   170→    \"\"\"Serializer for adding courses to cart\"\"\"\n   171→\n   172→    content_type_id = serializers.IntegerField()\n   173→    object_id = serializers.IntegerField()\n   174→    training_type = serializers.ChoiceField(choices=[\n   175→        ('masterclass', 'Masterclass'),\n   176→        ('learnership', 'Learnership'),\n   177→        ('industry_training', 'Industry Training'),\n   178→        ('custom_selection', 'Custom Selection'),\n   179→    ])\n   180→    from_wishlist = serializers.BooleanField(default=False)\n   181→\n   182→    def validate(self, data):\n   183→        \"\"\"Validate that the course exists\"\"\"\n   184→        try:\n   185→            content_type = ContentType.objects.get(id=data['content_type_id'])\n   186→            model_class = content_type.model_class()\n   187→            course = model_class.objects.get(id=data['object_id'])\n   188→\n   189→            # Store the course for later use\n   190→            self.context['course'] = course\n   191→            self.context['content_type'] = content_type\n   192→\n   193→        except ContentType.DoesNotExist:\n   194→            raise serializers.ValidationError(\"Invalid content type\")\n   195→        except model_class.DoesNotExist:\n   196→            raise serializers.ValidationError(\"Course not found\")\n   197→\n   198→        return data\n   199→\n   200→\n   201→class CourseProviderSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   202→    \"\"\"Serializer for course providers\"\"\"\n   203→\n   204→    active_courses_count = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   205→\n   206→    class Meta:\n   207→        model = CourseProvider\n   208→        fields = [\n   209→            'id', 'name', 'code', 'description', 'logo_url',\n   210→            'is_active', 'display_order', 'active_courses_count'\n   211→        ]\n   212→\n   213→    def get_active_courses_count(self, obj):\n   214→        \"\"\"Count active courses from this provider\"\"\"\n   215→        return CourseCatalogItem.objects.filter(\n   216→            provider=obj,\n   217→            is_active=True\n   218→        ).count()\n   219→\n   220→\n   221→class CourseCatalogItemSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   222→    \"\"\"Serializer for course catalog items\"\"\"\n   223→\n   224→    provider_name = serializers.CharField(source='provider.name', read_only=True)\n   225→    course_details = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   226→    in_wishlist = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   227→    in_cart = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   228→    \n   229→    # Localized Pricing (Shadowing model field)\n   230→    price = LocalizedPriceField(read_only=True)\n   231→    currency = CurrencyField(read_only=True)\n   232→    formatted_price = FormattedPriceField(source='*', price_field='price', read_only=True)\n   233→\n   234→    class Meta:\n   235→        model = CourseCatalogItem\n   236→        fields = [\n   237→            'id', 'content_type', 'object_id', 'training_type',\n   238→            'provider', 'provider_name', 'title', 'description',\n   239→            'price', 'currency', 'formatted_price',\n   240→            'is_active', 'is_featured',\n   241→            'display_order', 'total_enrollments', 'total_wishlist_adds',\n   242→            'course_details', 'in_wishlist', 'in_cart'\n   243→        ]\n   244→\n   245→    def get_course_details(self, obj):\n   246→        \"\"\"Get full course details\"\"\"\n   247→        if obj.course:\n   248→            return {\n   249→                'id': obj.object_id,\n   250→                'type': obj.content_type.model,\n   251→                'duration': getattr(obj.course, 'duration', ''),\n   252→                'level': getattr(obj.course, 'level', ''),\n   253→                'prerequisites': getattr(obj.course, 'prerequisites', []),\n   254→                'thumbnail_url': getattr(obj.course, 'thumbnail_url', ''),\n   255→            }\n   256→        return None\n   257→\n   258→    def get_in_wishlist(self, obj):\n   259→        \"\"\"Check if user has this course in wishlist\"\"\"\n   260→        user = self.context.get('request').user if self.context.get('request') else None\n   261→        if user and user.is_authenticated:\n   262→            return Wishlist.objects.filter(\n   263→                user=user,\n   264→                content_type=obj.content_type,\n   265→                object_id=obj.object_id\n   266→            ).exists()\n   267→        return False\n   268→\n   269→    def get_in_cart(self, obj):\n   270→        \"\"\"Check if user has this course in active cart\"\"\"\n   271→        user = self.context.get('request').user if self.context.get('request') else None\n   272→        if user and user.is_authenticated:\n   273→            active_cart = CourseCart.objects.filter(\n   274→                user=user,\n   275→                status='active'\n   276→            ).first()\n   277→            if active_cart:\n   278→                return CourseCartItem.objects.filter(\n   279→                    cart=active_cart,\n   280→                    content_type=obj.content_type,\n   281→                    object_id=obj.object_id\n   282→                ).exists()\n   283→        return False\n   284→\n   285→\n   286→# Cascading dropdown serializers\n   287→class CitySerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   288→    \"\"\"Serializer for cities in cascading dropdown\"\"\"\n   289→\n   290→    class Meta:\n   291→        model = City\n   292→        fields = ['id', 'name', 'state']\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→class StateSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   296→    \"\"\"Serializer for states in cascading dropdown\"\"\"\n   297→\n   298→    cities = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   299→\n   300→    class Meta:\n   301→        model = State\n   302→        fields = ['id', 'name', 'code', 'country', 'cities']\n   303→\n   304→    def get_cities(self, obj):\n   305→        \"\"\"Optionally include cities if requested\"\"\"\n   306→        if self.context.get('include_cities'):\n   307→            cities = obj.cities.all().order_by('name')\n   308→            return CitySerializer(cities, many=True).data\n   309→        return None\n   310→\n   311→\n   312→class CountrySerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):\n   313→    \"\"\"Serializer for countries in cascading dropdown\"\"\"\n   314→\n   315→    states = serializers.SerializerMethodField()\n   316→\n   317→    class Meta:\n   318→        model = Country\n   319→        fields = ['id', 'name', 'code', 'phone_code', 'states']\n   320→\n   321→    def get_states(self, obj):\n   322→        \"\"\"Optionally include states if requested\"\"\"\n   323→        if self.context.get('include_states'):\n   324→            states = obj.states.all().order_by('name')\n   325→            return StateSerializer(states, many=True).data\n   326→        return None\n   327→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should successful payment\"\"\"\n    48→        profile, created = StudentProfile.objects.get_or_create(user=request.user)\n    49→\n    50→        company_details = request.data.get('company_details', {})\n    51→        profile.update_company_details(company_details)\n    52→\n    53→        serializer = self.get_serializer(profile)\n    54→        return Response(serializer.data)\n    55→\n    56→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n    57→    def company_details(self, request):\n    58→        \"\"\"Get previous company details for reuse\"\"\"\n    59→        try:\n    60→            profile = StudentProfile.objects.get(user=request.user)\n    61→            if profile.has_company_payment_history:\n    62→                return Response({\n    63→                    'has_history': True,\n    64→                    'details': profile.get_company_details()\n    65→                })\n    66→            return Response({'has_history': False})\n    67→        except StudentProfile.DoesNotExist:\n    68→            return Response({'has_history': False})\n    69→\n    70→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n    71→    def check_existing_student(self, request):\n    72→        \"\"\"\n    73→        Check if user is an existing student with prior enrollments.\n    74→        Returns student data if they exist, used to skip personal info collection.\n    75→        \"\"\"\n    76→        from apps.payments.models import Enrollment\n    77→        \n    78→        try:\n    79→            profile = StudentProfile.objects.get(user=request.user)\n    80→            \n    81→            # Check if user has any completed enrollments\n    82→            has_enrollments = Enrollment.objects.filter(\n    83→                user=request.user,\n    84→                status__in=['enrolled', 'completed']\n    85→            ).exists()\n    86→            \n    87→            if has_enrollments:\n    88→                return Response({\n    89→                    'is_existing_student': True,\n    90→                    'student_data': {\n    91→                        'name': request.user.get_full_name() or request.user.username,\n    92→                        'email': request.user.email,\n    93→                        'phone': profile.phone or '',\n    94→ get_permissions(self):\n   119→        \"\"\"Allow guest users to check wishlist status without 403\"\"\"\n   120→        if self.action in ['list', 'unconverted']:\n   121→            return [AllowAny()]\n   122→        return super().get_permissions()\n   123→\n   124→    def get_queryset(self):\n   125→        \"\"\"Filter to current user's wishlist\"\"\"\n   126→        if not self.request.user.is_authenticated:\n   127→            return Wishlist.objects.none()\n   128→        return Wishlist.objects.filter(user=self.request.user).order_by('-created_at')\n   129→\n   130→    def perform_create(self, serializer):\n   131→        \"\"\"Auto-assign current user\"\"\"\n   132→        serializer.save(user=self.request.user)\n   133→\n   134→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'])\n   135→    def move_to_cart(self, request, pk=None):\n   136→        \"\"\"Move wishlist item to cart\"\"\"\n   137→        wishlist_item = self.get_object()\n   138→\n   139→ defaults={'total_courses': 0, 'total_amount': 0}\n   144→        )\n   145→\n   146→        # Check if already in cart\n   147→        existing_item = CourseCartItem.objects.filter(\n   148→            cart=cart,\n   149→            content_type=wishlist_item.content_type,\n   150→            object_id=wishlist_item.object_id\n   151→        ).first()\n   152→\n   153→        if existing_item:\n   154→            return Response(\n   155→                {'error': 'Course already in cart'},\n   156→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST\n   157→            )\n   158→\n   159→ CourseCartItem.objects.create(\n   165→            cart=cart,\n   166→            content_type=wishlist_item.content_type,\n   167→            object_id=wishlist_item.object_id,\n   168→            training_type=wishlist_item.training_type,\n   169→            price=price,\n   170→            added_from_wishlist=True\n   171→        )\n   172→\n   173→        # Mark wishlist item as converted\n   174→        wishlist_item.converted_to_cart = True\n   175→        wishlist_item.save()\n   176→\n   177→        # Recalculate cart totals\n   178→        cart.recalculate_totals()\n   179→\n   180→        return Response({\n   181→            'message': 'Course moved to cart',\n   182→            'cart_item_id': cart_item.id,\n   183→            'cart_id': cart.id\n   184→        })\n   185→\n   186→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n   187→    def by_training_type(self, request):\n   188→        \"\"\"Get wishlist items grouped by training type\"\"\"\n   189→        training_type = request.query_params.get('type')\n   190→        queryset = self.get_queryset()\n   191→\n   192→        if training_type:\n   193→            queryset = queryset.filter(training_type=training_type)\n   194→\n   195→        serializer = self.get_serializer(queryset, many=True)\n   196→        return Response(serializer.data)\n   197→\n   198→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n   199→ Response(serializer.data)\n   207→\n   208→\n   209→class CourseCartViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n   210→    \"\"\"\n   211→    ViewSet for managing course cart and checkout\n   212→    \"\"\"\n   213→    serializer_class = CourseCartSerializer\n   214→    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]\n   215→\n   216→    def get_permissions(self):\n   217→        \"\"\"Allow guest users to check active cart status without 403\"\"\"\n   218→        if self.action == 'active':\n   219→            return [AllowAny()]\n   220→        return super().get_permissions()\n   221→\n   222→\n   223→    def get_queryset(self):\n   224→        \"\"\"Filter to current user's carts\"\"\"\n   225→        if not self.request.user.is_authenticated:\n   226→            return CourseCart.objects.none()\n   227→        return CourseCart.objects.filter(user=self.request.user).order_by('-created_at')\n   228→\n   229→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n   230→    def active(self, request):\n   231→        \"\"\"Get or create active cart\"\"\"\n   232→        if not request.user.is_authenticated:\n   233→            # Return a mock empty cart structure for guests\n   234→            return Response({\n   235→                'id': None,\n   236→                'status': 'active',\n   237→                'total_courses': 0,\n   238→                'total_amount': \"0.00\",\n   239→                'currency': 'USD',\n   240→                'items': [],\n   241→                'is_guest': True\n   242→            })\n   243→\n   244→        cart, created = CourseCart.objects.get_or_create(\n   245→            user=request.user,\n   246→            status='active',\n   247→            defaults={'total_courses': 0, 'total_amount': 0}\n   248→        )\n   249→        serializer = self.get_serializer(cart)\n   250→        return Response(serializer.data)\n   251→\n   252→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'])\n   253→    def add_item(self, request, pk=None):\n   254→        \"\"\"Add course to cart (drag-and-drop from catalog)\"\"\"\n   255→        cart = self.get_object()\n   256→\n   257→        serializer = AddToCartSerializer(\n   258→            data=request.data,\n   259→            context={'request': request}\n   260→        )\n   261→        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n   262→\n   263→        # Get validated course and content type from context\n   264→        course = serializer.context['course']\n   265→        content_type = serializer.context['content_type']\n   266→\n   267→        # Check if already in cart\n   268→        existing_item = CourseCartItem.objects.filter(\n   269→            cart=cart,\n   270→            content_type=content_type,\n   271→            object_id=serializer.validated_data['object_id']\n   272→        ).exists()\n   273→\n   274→        if existing_item:\n   275→            return Response(\n   276→                {'error': 'Course already in cart'},\n   277→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST\n   278→            )\n   279→\n   280→        # Get price from course\n   281→        price = getattr(course, 'price', 0)\n   282→\n   283→        # Create cart item\n   284→        cart_item = CourseCartItem.objects.create(\n   285→            cart=cart,\n   286→            content_type=content_type,\n   287→            object_id=serializer.validated_data['object_id'],\n   288→            training_type=serializer.validated_data['training_type'],\n   289→            price=price,\n   290→            added_from_wishlist=serializer.validated_data.get('from_wishlist', False)\n   291→        )\n   292→\n   293→        # If from wishlist, mark wishlist item as converted\n   294→        if serializer.validated_data.get('from_wishlist'):\n   295→            Wishlist.objects.filter(\n   296→                user=request.user,\n   297→                content_type=content_type,\n   298→                object_id=serializer.validated_data['object_id']\n   299→            ).update(converted_to_cart=True)\n   300→\n   301→        # Recalculate cart totals\n   302→        cart.recalculate_totals()\n   303→\n   304→        serializer = CourseCartItemSerializer(cart_item)\n   305→        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)\n   306→\n   307→    @action(detail=True, methods=['delete'])\n   308→    def remove_item(self, request, pk=None):\n   309→        \"\"\"Remove course from cart\"\"\"\n   310→        cart = self.get_object()\n   311→        item_id = request.data.get('item_id')\n   312→\n   313→        try:\n   314→            cart_item = CourseCartItem.objects.get(id=item_id, cart=cart)\n   315→            cart_item.delete()\n   316→            cart.recalculate_totals()\n   317→            return Response({'message': 'Item removed from cart'})\n   318→        except CourseCartItem.DoesNotExist:\n   319→            return Response(\n   320→                {'error': 'Cart item not found'},\n   321→                status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND\n   322→            )\n   323→\n   324→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'])\n   325→    def clear(self, request, pk=None):\n   326→        \"\"\"Clear all items from cart\"\"\"\n   327→        cart = self.get_object()\n   328→        cart.items.all().delete()\n   329→        cart.recalculate_totals()\n   330→        return Response({'message': 'Cart cleared'})\n   331→\n   332→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'])\n   333→    def checkout(self, request, pk=None):\n   334→        \"\"\"Initiate checkout process\"\"\"\n   335→        cart = self.get_object()\n   336→\n   337→        if cart.items.count() == 0:\n   338→            return Response(\n   339→                {'error': 'Cannot checkout empty cart'},\n   340→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST\n   341→            )\n   342→\n   343→        # Update cart status\n   344→        cart.status = 'checkout'\n   345→        cart.save()\n   346→\n   347→        # Get company details preference\n   348→        use_previous_details = request.data.get('use_previous_company_details', False)\n   349→        is_corporate = request.data.get('is_corporate_enrollment', False)\n   350→\n   351→        cart.use_previous_company_details = use_previous_details\n   352→        cart.is_corporate_enrollment = is_corporate\n   353→        cart.save()\n   354→\n   355→        # Create Order for payment system\n   356→        course_ids = list(cart.items.values_list('object_id', flat=True))\n   357→        \n   358→        order = Order.objects.create(\n   359→            user=request.user,\n   360→            tracking=f\"CART-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12].upper()}\",\n   361→            amount=cart.total_amount,\n   362→            currency=cart.currency,\n   363→            status='pending',\n   364→            metadata={\n   365→                'cart_id': cart.id,\n   366→                'enrollment_type': 'custom_selection',\n   367→                'is_corporate': is_corporate,\n   368→                'use_previous_details': use_previous_details,\n   369→                'course_ids': course_ids,\n   370→                'total_courses': cart.total_courses,\n   371→            }\n   372→        )\n   373→\n   374→        return Response({\n   375→            'message': 'Cart ready for checkout',\n   376→            'cart_id': cart.id,\n   377→            'order_id': order.tracking,\n   378→            'total_amount': str(cart.total_amount),\n   379→            'currency': cart.currency,\n   380→            'total_courses': cart.total_courses,\n   381→            'proceed_to_payment': True\n   382→        })\n   383→\n   384→\n   385→class CourseCatalogViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   386→    \"\"\"\n   387→    ViewSet for browsing course catalog (custom selection enrollment)\n   388→    Only shows courses from active providers\n   389→    \"\"\"\n   390→    serializer_class = CourseCatalogItemSerializer\n   391→    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]\n   392→\n   393→    def get_queryset(self):\n   394→        \"\"\"Get active courses from active providers\"\"\"\n   395→        return CourseCatalogItem.objects.filter(\n   396→            is_active=True,\n   397→            provider__is_active=True\n   398→        ).select_related('provider', 'content_type').order_by('display_order', '-is_featured')\n   399→\n   400→    def list(self, request):\n   401→        \"\"\"List catalog with filters\"\"\"\n   402→        queryset = self.get_queryset()\n   403→\n   404→        # Filter by training type\n   405→        training_type = request.query_params.get('training_type')\n   406→        if training_type:\n   407→            queryset = queryset.filter(training_type=training_type)\n   408→\n   409→        # Filter by provider\n   410→        provider_id = request.query_params.get('provider')\n   411→        if provider_id:\n   412→            queryset = queryset.filter(provider_id=provider_id)\n   413→\n   414→        # Filter by featured\n   415→        featured = request.query_params.get('featured')\n   416→        if featured == 'true':\n   417→            queryset = queryset.filter(is_featured=True)\n   418→\n   419→        # Search by title\n   420→        search = request.query_params.get('search')\n   421→        if search:\n   422→            queryset = queryset.filter(\n   423→                Q(title__icontains=search) |\n   424→                Q(description__icontains=search)\n   425→            )\n   426→\n   427→        serializer = self.get_serializer(queryset, many=True)\n   428→        return Response(serializer.data)\n   429→\n   430→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'])\n   431→    def by_training_type(self, request):\n   432→        \"\"\"Get catalog items grouped by training type\"\"\"\n   433→        training_type = request.query_params.get('type')\n   434→        if not training_type:\n   435→            return Response(\n   436→                {'error': 'training_type parameter required'},\n   437→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST\n   438→            )\n   439→\n   440→        queryset = self.get_queryset().filter(training_type=training_type)\n   441→        serializer = self.get_serializer(queryset, many=True)\n   442→        return Response(serializer.data)\n   443→\n   444→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'])\n   445→    def add_to_wishlist(self, request, pk=None):\n   446→        \"\"\"Add catalog item to wishlist\"\"\"\n   447→        catalog_item = self.get_object()\n   448→\n   449→        # Check if already in wishlist\n   450→        existing = Wishlist.objects.filter(\n   451→            user=request.user,\n   452→            content_type=catalog_item.content_type,\n   453→            object_id=catalog_item.object_id\n   454→        ).exists()\n   455→\n   456→        if existing:\n   457→            return Response(\n   458→                {'error': 'Course already in wishlist'},\n   459→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST\n   460→            )\n   461→\n   462→        # Create wishlist item\n   463→        wishlist_item = Wishlist.objects.create(\n   464→            user=request.user,\n   465→            content_type=catalog_item.content_type,\n   466→            object_id=catalog_item.object_id,\n   467→            training_type=catalog_item.training_type,\n   468→            interest_level='medium',\n   469→            notes=request.data.get('reason', '')  # specific reason\n   470→        )\n   471→\n   472→        # Update catalog item stats\n   473→        catalog_item.total_wishlist_adds += 1\n   474→        catalog_item.save()\n   475→\n   476→        serializer = create active cart\n   485→        cart, created = CourseCart.objects.get_or_create(\n   486→            user=request.user,\n   487→            status='active',\n   488→ status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)\n   518→\n   519→\n   520→class CourseProviderViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   521→    \"\"\"\n   522→    ViewSet for browsing course providers\n   523→    Only shows active providers in custom enrollment catalog\n   524→    \"\"\"\n   525→    serializer_class = CourseProviderSerializer\n   526→    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]\n   527→\n   528→    def get_queryset(self):\n   529→        \"\"\"Get only active providers\"\"\"\n   530→        return CourseProvider.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by('display_order')\n   531→\n   532→    @action(detail=True, methods=['get'])\n   533→    def courses(self, request, pk=None):\n   534→        \"\"\"Get all courses from this provider\"\"\"\n   535→        provider = self.get_object()\n CountryViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   547→    \"\"\"\n   548→    ViewSet for countries in cascading dropdown\n   549→    \"\"\"\n   550→    serializer_class = CountrySerializer\n   551→ if requested\"\"\"\n   556→        context = super().get_serializer_context()\n   557→        context['include_states'] = self.request.query_params.get('include_states') == 'true'\n   558→        return context\n   559→\n   560→\n   561→class StateViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   562→    \"\"\"\n   563→    ViewSet for states in cascading dropdown\n   564→    Filter by country\n   565→    \"\"\"\n   566→    serializer_class = StateSerializer\n   567→    permission_classes = [AllowAny]\n   568→\n   569→    def get_queryset(self):\n   570→        \"\"\"Filter by country if provided\"\"\"\n   571→        queryset = State.objects.all().order_by('name')\n   572→\n   573→        country_id = self.request.query_params.get('country')\n   574→        country_code = self.request.query_params.get('country_code')\n   575→\n   576→        if country_id:\n   577→            queryset = queryset.filter(country_id=country_id)\n   578→        elif country_code:\n   579→            queryset = queryset.filter(country__code=country_code)\n   580→\n   581→        return queryset\n   582→\n   583→    def get_serializer_context(self):\n   584→        \"\"\"Add flag to include cities if requested\"\"\"\n   585→        context = super().get_serializer_context()\n   586→        context['include_cities'] = self.request.query_params.get('include_cities') == 'true'\n   587→        return context\n   588→\n   589→\n   590→class CityViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   591→    \"\"\"\n   592→    ViewSet for cities in cascading dropdown\n   593→    Filter by state\n   594→    \"\"\"\n   595→    serializer_class = CitySerializer\n   596→    permission_classes = [AllowAny]\n   597→\n   598→    def get_queryset(self):\n   599→        \"\"\"Filter by state if provided\"\"\"\n   600→        queryset = City.objects.all().order_by('name')\n   601→\n   602→        state_id = self.request.query_params.get('state')\n   603→        country_id = self.request.query_params.get('country')\n   604→\n   605→        if state_id:\n   606→            queryset = queryset.filter(state_id=state_id)\n   607→        elif country_id:\n   608→            queryset = queryset.filter(state__country_id=country_id)\n   609→\n   610→        return queryset\n   611→\n   612→\n   613→@api_view(['GET'])\n   614→@permission_classes([AllowAny])\n   615→def get_content_types(request):\n   616→    \"\"\"\n   617→    Return content type IDs for course models.\n   618→\n   619→    This endpoint helps the frontend determine the correct content_type_id\n   620→    to use when adding items to cart/wishlist.\n   621→\n   622→    Returns:\n   623→        {\n   624→            \"masterclass\": 12,\n   625→            \"learnershipprogramme\": 13,\n   626→            \"aicertscourse\": 14,\n   627→            \"offering\": 15,\n   628→            \"course\": 16\n   629→        }\n   630→    \"\"\"\n   631→    # Specify app_label explicitly to avoid MultipleObjectsReturned when\n   632→    # the same model name exists in multiple apps (e.g. 'course', 'aicertscourse').\n   633→    model_lookup = [\n   634→        ('masterclass',         'masterclasses'),\n   635→        ('learnershipprogramme','learnerships'),\n   636→        ('aicertscourse',       'aicerts_courses'),\n   637→        ('offering',            'industry_based_training'),\n   638→        ('course',              'courses'),\n   639→    ]\n   640→\n   641→    content_types = {}\n   642→    for model_name, app_label in model_lookup:\n   643→        try:\n   644→            ct = ContentType.objects.get(app_label=app_label, model=model_name)\n   645→            content_types[model_name] = ct.id\n   646→        except ContentType.DoesNotExist:\n   647→            content_types[model_name] = None\n   648→\n   649→    return Response(content_types)\n   650→\n   651→\n   652→@api_view(['GET'])\n   653→@permission_classes([IsAuthenticated])\n   654→def get_marketing_analytics(request):\n   655→    \"\"\"\n   656→    Sales & Marketing analytics endpoint.\n   657→\n   658→    Returns:\n   659→    - top_wishlist: courses sorted by wishlist count (highest first)\n   660→    - top_sales: courses sorted by enrollment count (highest first)\n   661→    - low_sales: courses with lowest enrollment (but at least 1)\n   662→    - wishlist_summary: total wishlist items, conversion rate\n   663→    \"\"\"\n   664→    limit = int(request.query_params.get('limit', 10))\n   665→\n   666→    # ── Wishlist analytics: count per course using Wishlist table ──\n   667→    wishlist_counts = (\n   668→        Wishlist.objects\n   669→        .values('object_id', 'training_type', 'content_type_id')\n   670→        .annotate(wishlist_count=Count('id'))\n   671→        .order_by('-wishlist_count')\n   672→    )\n   673→\n   674→    # Build top wishlist list with title lookup\n   675→    top_wishlist = []\n   676→    seen_ids = set()\n   677→    for row in wishlist_counts:\n   678→        if len(top_wishlist) >= limit:\n   679→            break\n   680→        key = (row['content_type_id'], row['object_id'])\n   681→        if key in seen_ids:\n   682→            continue\n   683→        seen_ids.add(key)\n   684→        try:\n   685→            ct = ContentType.objects.get(id=row['content_type_id'])\n   686→            obj = ct.get_object_for_this_type(pk=row['object_id'])\n   687→            title = getattr(obj, 'title', getattr(obj, 'name', str(obj)))\n   688→        except Exception:\n   689→            title = f\"[{row['training_type']}] ID {row['object_id']}\"\n   690→        top_wishlist.append({\n   691→            'title': title,\n   692→            'training_type': row['training_type'],\n   693→            'object_id': row['object_id'],\n   694→            'wishlist_count': row['wishlist_count'],\n   695→            'converted_count': Wishlist.objects.filter(\n   696→                content_type_id=row['content_type_id'],\n   697→                object_id=row['object_id'],\n   698→                converted_to_cart=True,\n   699→            ).count(),\n   700→        })\n   701→\n   702→    # ── Sales analytics: count enrollments per course via Enrollment model ──\n   703→    from apps.payments.models import Enrollment\n   704→\n   705→    enrollment_counts = (\n   706→        Enrollment.objects\n   707→        .values('content_type_id', 'object_id', 'enrollment_type')\n   708→        .annotate(enrollment_count=Count('id'))\n   709→        .order_by('-enrollment_count')\n   710→    )\n   711→\n   712→    top_sales = []\n   713→    seen_enroll = set()\n   714→    for row in list(enrollment_counts):\n   715→        if len(top_sales) >= limit * 2:\n   716→            break\n   717→        key = (row['content_type_id'], row['object_id'])\n   718→        if key in seen_enroll:\n   719→            continue\n   720→        seen_enroll.add(key)\n   721→        title = f\"[{row.get('enrollment_type', 'unknown')}] ID {row['object_id']}\"\n   722→        try:\n   723→            ct = ContentType.objects.get(id=row['content_type_id'])\n   724→            obj = ct.get_object_for_this_type(pk=row['object_id'])\n   725→            title = getattr(obj, 'title', getattr(obj, 'name', title))\n   726→        except Exception:\n   727→            pass\n   728→        top_sales.append({\n   729→            'object_id': row['object_id'],\n   730→            'training_type': row.get('enrollment_type', 'unknown'),\n   731→            'enrollment_count': row['enrollment_count'],\n   732→            'title': title,\n   733→        })\n   734→\n   735→    # Reverse for lowest sales (exclude zero enrollments from top_sales)\n   736→    low_sales = sorted(\n   737→        [e for e in top_sales if e['enrollment_count'] > 0],\n   738→        key=lambda x: x['enrollment_count']\n   739→    )[:limit]\n   740→\n   741→    # ── Wishlist summary stats ──\n   742→    total_wishlist = Wishlist.objects.count()\n   743→    converted_to_cart = Wishlist.objects.filter(converted_to_cart=True).count()\n   744→    converted_to_enrollment = Wishlist.objects.filter(converted_to_enrollment=True).count()\n   745→    conversion_rate = round(\n   746→        (converted_to_enrollment / total_wishlist * 100) if total_wishlist > 0 else 0, 1\n   747→    )\n   748→\n   749→    # Training type breakdown for wishlist\n   750→    type_breakdown = list(\n   751→        Wishlist.objects\n   752→        .values('training_type')\n   753→        .annotate(count=Count('id'))\n   754→        .order_by('-count')\n   755→    )\n   756→\n   757→    return Response({\n   758→        'top_wishlist': top_wishlist,\n   759→        'top_sales': top_sales[:limit],\n   760→        'low_sales': low_sales,\n   761→        'wishlist_summary': {\n   762→            'total': total_wishlist,\n   763→            'converted_to_cart': converted_to_cart,\n   764→            'converted_to_enrollment': converted_to_enrollment,\n   765→            'conversion_rate_percent': conversion_rate,\n   766→            'by_training_type': type_breakdown,\n   767→        },\n   768→    })\n   769→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. import gettext_lazy as _\n     6→from django.contrib.contenttypes.fields import GenericForeignKey\n     7→from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType\n     8→\n     9→\n    10→class StudentProfile(models.Model):\n    11→    \"\"\"\n    12→    Extended student profile to store enrollment history and payment preferences.\n    13→\n    14→    Tracks:\n    15→    - Previous company details (for quick reuse)\n    16→    - Previous individual details\n    17→    - Payment method preferences\n    18→    - Enrollment history\n    19→    \"\"\"\n    20→\n    21→    user = models.OneToOneField(\n    22→        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,\n    23→        on_delete=models.CASCADE,\n    24→        related_name='student_profile',\n    25→        verbose_name=_(\"User\")\n    26→    )\n    27→\n    28→    # Previous Company Details (for reuse)\n    29→    last_used_company_name = models.CharField(\n    30→        max_length=255,\n    31→        blank=True,\n    32→        verbose_name=_(\"Last Used Company Name\")\n    33→    )\n    34→    last_used_company_email = models.EmailField(\n    35→        blank=True,\n    36→        verbose_name=_(\"Last Used Company Email\")\n    37→    )\n    38→    last_used_company_phone = models.CharField(\n    39→        max_length=50,\n    40→        blank=True,\n    41→        verbose_name=_(\"Last Used Company Phone\")\n    42→    )\n    43→    last_used_company_address = models.TextField(\n    44→        blank=True,\n    45→        verbose_name=_(\"Last Used Company Address\")\n    46→    )\n    47→    last_used_vat_number = models.CharField(\n    48→        max_length=50,\n    49→        blank=True,\n    50→        verbose_name=_(\"Last Used VAT Number\")\n    51→    )\n    52→    has_company_payment_history = models.BooleanField(\n    53→        default=False,\n    54→        verbose_name=_(\"Has Used Company Payment Before\"),\n    55→        help_text=_(\"True if student has paid via company before\")\n    56→    )\n    57→\n    58→    # Location Preferences\n    59→    preferred_country = models.ForeignKey(\n    60→        'localization.Country',\n    61→        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,\n    62→        null=True,\n    63→        blank=True,\n    64→        related_name='preferred_students',\n    65→        verbose_name=_(\"Preferred Country\")\n    66→    )\n    67→    preferred_state = models.ForeignKey(\n    68→        'localization.State',\n    69→        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,\n    70→        null=True,\n    71→        blank=True,\n    72→        related_name='preferred_students',\n    73→        verbose_name=_(\"Preferred State/Region\")\n    74→    )\n    75→    preferred_city = models.ForeignKey(\n    76→        'localization.City',\n    77→        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,\n    78→        null=True,\n    79→        blank=True,\n    80→        related_name='preferred_students',\n    81→        verbose_name=_(\"Preferred City\")\n    82→    )\n    83→\n    84→    # Payment Preferences\n    85→    preferred_payment_provider = models.CharField(\n    86→        max_length=50,\n    87→        blank=True,\n    88→        verbose_name=_(\"Preferred Payment Provider\"),\n    89→        help_text=_(\"Last used payment provider (paystack, flutterwave, etc.)\")\n    90→    )\n    91→    preferred_payment_method = models.CharField(\n    92→        max_length=20,\n    93→        blank=True,\n    94→        verbose_name=_(\"Preferred Payment Method\"),\n    95→        help_text=_(\"Last used payment method (card, mobile_money, bank_transfer)\")\n    96→    )\n    97→\n    98→    # Enrollment Stats\n    99→    total_enrollments = models.IntegerField(\n   100→        default=0,\n   101→        verbose_name=_(\"Total Enrollments\")\n   102→    )\n   103→    active_enrollments = models.IntegerField(\n   104→        default=0,\n   105→        verbose_name=_(\"Active Enrollments\")\n   106→    )\n   107→    completed_enrollments = models.IntegerField(\n   108→        default=0,\n   109→        verbose_name=_(\"Completed Enrollments\")\n   110→    )\n   111→\n   112→    # Metadata\n   113→    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)\n   114→    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)\n   115→\n   116→    class Meta:\n   117→        db_table = 'learner_profiles'  # Keep DB table name for data continuity\n   118→        verbose_name = _(\"Student Profile\")\n   119→        verbose_name_plural = _(\"Student Profiles\")\n   120→\n   121→    def __str__(self):\n   122→        return f\"Profile: {self.user.email}\"\n   123→\n   124→    def get_company_details(self):\n   125→        \"\"\"Get last used company details for reuse\"\"\"\n   126→        if not self.has_company_payment_history:\n   127→            return None\n   128→\n   129→        return {\n   130→            'company_name': self.last_used_company_name,\n   131→            'contact_email': self.last_used_company_email,\n   132→            'contact_phone': self.last_used_company_phone,\n   133→            'company_address': self.last_used_company_address,\n   134→            'vat_number': self.last_used_vat_number,\n   135→        }\n   136→\n   137→    def update_company_details(self, company_details):\n   138→        \"\"\"Update stored company details after successful payment\"\"\"\n   139→        self.last_used_company_name = company_details.get('company_name', '')\n   140→        self.last_used_company_email = company_details.get('contact_email', '')\n   141→        self.last_used_company_phone = company_details.get('contact_phone', '')\n   142→        self.last_used_company_address = company_details.get('company_address', '')\n   143→        self.last_used_vat_number = company_details.get('vat_number', '')\n   144→        self.has_company_payment_history = True\n   145→        self.save()\n   146→\n   147→\n   148→class Wishlist(models.Model):\n   149→    \"\"\"\n   150→    Student wishlist for courses they're interested in but not yet enrolled.\n   151→\n   152→    Features:\n   153→    - Tracks all 4 training types (Masterclass, Learnership, Industry, Custom)\n   154→    - Available to Marketing team for lead follow-up\n   155→    - Shows courses student wants to study (now or future)\n   156→    - Excludes courses already enrolled in\n   157→    \"\"\"\n   158→\n   159→    TRAINING_TYPE_CHOICES = [\n   160→        ('masterclass', 'Masterclass'),\n   161→        ('learnership', 'Learnership'),\n   162→        ('industry_training', 'Industry Training'),\n   163→        ('custom_selection', 'Custom Selection'),\n   164→    ]\n   165→\n   166→    INTEREST_LEVEL_CHOICES = [\n   167→        ('high', 'High - Want to enroll soon'),\n   168→        ('medium', 'Medium - Considering'),\n   169→        ('low', 'Low - Just browsing'),\n   170→    ]\n   171→\n   172→    user = models.ForeignKey(\n   173→        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,\n   174→        on_delete=models.CASCADE,\n   175→        related_name='wishlist_items',\n   176→        verbose_name=_(\"Student\")\n   177→    )\n   178→\n   179→    # Generic relation to any course type\n   180→    content_type = models.ForeignKey(\n   181→        ContentType,\n   182→        on_delete=models.CASCADE,\n   183→        limit_choices_to={\n   184→            'model__in': (\n   185→                'masterclass',\n   186→                'learnershipprogramme',\n   187→                'aicertscourse',\n   188→                'offering',  # Industry training\n   189→                'course'  # Generic course\n   190→            )\n   191→        },\n   192→        verbose_name=_(\"Course Type\")\n   193→    )\n   194→    object_id = models.PositiveIntegerField(verbose_name=_(\"Course ID\"))\n   195→    course = GenericForeignKey('content_type', 'object_id')\n   196→\n   197→    # Training type for filtering\n   198→    training_type = models.CharField(\n   199→        max_length=20,\n   200→        choices=TRAINING_TYPE_CHOICES,\n   201→        verbose_name=_(\"Training Type\")\n   202→    )\n   203→\n   204→    # Interest tracking\n   205→    interest_level = models.CharField(\n   206→        max_length=10,\n   207→        choices=INTEREST_LEVEL_CHOICES,\n   208→        default='medium',\n   209→        verbose_name=_(\"Interest Level\")\n   210→    )\n   211→\n   212→    # Timeline\n   213→    intended_start = models.CharField(\n   214→        max_length=20,\n   215→        choices=[\n   216→            ('immediate', 'Immediate (within 1 month)'),\n   217→            ('short_term', 'Short term (1-3 months)'),\n   218→            ('medium_term', 'Medium term (3-6 months)'),\n   219→            ('long_term', 'Long term (6+ months)'),\n   220→            ('undecided', 'Undecided'),\n   221→        ],\n   222→        default='undecided',\n   223→        verbose_name=_(\"Intended Start Time\")\n   224→    )\n   225→\n   226→    # User notes (Reason)\n   227→    notes = models.TextField(\n   228→        blank=True,\n   229→        verbose_name=_(\"Reason for Interest\"),\n   230→        help_text=_(\"Why does the student want this course?\")\n   231→    )\n   232→\n   233→    # Marketing follow-up\n   234→    marketing_contacted = models.BooleanField(\n   235→        default=False,\n   236→        verbose_name=_(\"Marketing Contacted\"),\n   237→        help_text=_(\"Has marketing team followed up on this lead?\")\n   238→    )\n   239→    marketing_contacted_at = models.DateTimeField(\n   240→        null=True,\n   241→        blank=True,\n   242→        verbose_name=_(\"Marketing Contact Date\")\n   243→    )\n   244→    marketing_notes = models.TextField(\n   245→        blank=True,\n   246→        verbose_name=_(\"Marketing Notes\"),\n   247→        help_text=_(\"Notes from marketing follow-up\")\n   248→    )\n   249→    contacted_by = models.ForeignKey(\n   250→        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,\n   251→        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,\n   252→        null=True,\n   253→        blank=True,\n   254→        related_name='contacted_wishlist_leads',\n   255→        verbose_name=_(\"Contacted By\")\n   256→    )\n   257→\n   258→    # Conversion tracking\n   259→    converted_to_cart = models.BooleanField(\n   260→        default=False,\n   261→        verbose_name=_(\"Moved to Cart\"),\n   262→        help_text=_(\"Did student add this to their cart?\")\n   263→    )\n   264→    converted_to_cart_at = models.DateTimeField(\n   265→        null=True,\n   266→        blank=True,\n   267→        verbose_name=_(\"Added to Cart At\")\n   268→    )\n   269→\n   270→    converted_to_enrollment = models.BooleanField(\n   271→        default=False,\n   272→        verbose_name=_(\"Enrolled\"),\n   273→        help_text=_(\"Did student complete enrollment?\")\n   274→    )\n   275→    converted_to_enrollment_at = models.DateTimeField(\n   276→        null=True,\n   277→        blank=True,\n   278→        verbose_name=_(\"Enrolled At\")\n   279→    )\n   280→\n   281→    # Metadata\n   282→    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True, verbose_name=_(\"Added to Wishlist\"))\n   283→    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)\n   284→\n   285→    class Meta:\n   286→        db_table = 'wishlist'\n   287→        verbose_name = _(\"Wishlist Item\")\n   288→        verbose_name_plural = _(\"Wishlist\")\n   289→        ordering = ['-created_at']\n     1→// lib/src/core/api/api_client.dart\n     2→\n     3→import 'package:dio/dio.dart';\n     4→import 'package:pretty_dio_logger/pretty_dio_logger.dart';\n     5→\n     6→import '../config/environment.dart';\n     7→import '../services/auth_service.dart';\n     8→import '../../data/models/masterclass.dart';\n     9→import '../../data/models/course.dart';\n    10→import '../../data/models/learnership.dart';\n    11→\n    12→class ApiClient {\n    13→  static final Dio _dio = Dio(\n    14→    BaseOptions(\n    15→      baseUrl: Environment.apiBaseUrl,\n    16→      connectTimeout: Duration(seconds: Environment.apiTimeout),\n    17→      receiveTimeout: Duration(seconds: Environment.apiTimeout),\n    18→      headers: {\n    19→        'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n    20→        'Accept': 'application/json',\n    21→      },\n    22→    ),\n    23→  );\n    24→\n    25→  static void initialize() {\n    26→    // Add Authentication Interceptor\n    27→    _dio.interceptors.add(\n    28→      InterceptorsWrapper(\n    29→        onRequest: (options, handler) async {\n    30→          // Skip auth header for login endpoint to prevent potential issues\n    31→          if (!options.path.contains('/auth/login')) {\n    32→            final token = await AuthService.getAccessToken();\n    33→            if (token != null && token.isNotEmpty) {\n    34→              options.headers['Authorization'] = 'Bearer $token';\n    35→            }\n    36→          }\n    37→          return handler.next(options);\n    38→        },\n    39→      ),\n    40→    );\n    41→\n    42→    _dio.interceptors.add(\n    43→      PrettyDioLogger(\n    44→        requestHeader: true,\n    45→        requestBody: true,\n    46→ {"parentUuid":"043e0c63-c256-4020-bbd5-16b6532c5772","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"progress","data":{"message":{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DBC6oAUHf8MdeWzwJS4wcg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# apps/aicerts_integration/views.py\n     2→\"\"\"\n     3→Views for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n     4→\n     5→Handles:\n     6→- Course enrollment with dual sync (Hosi + AICERTs)\n     7→- SSO redirect generation for course access\n     8→- Instructor validation for course assignments\n     9→- Enrollment status tracking\n    10→\"\"\"\n    11→\n    12→from rest_framework import viewsets, status, permissions\n    13→from rest_framework.decorators import action\n    14→from rest_framework.response import Response\n    15→from rest_framework.views import APIView\n    16→from django.shortcuts import redirect, get_object_or_404\n    17→from django.http import JsonResponse\n    18→from django.utils import timezone\n    19→from django.db import transaction\n    20→from django.conf import settings\n    21→import logging\n    22→import secrets\n    23→from datetime import timedelta\n    24→\n    25→from .models import (\n    26→    AICertsEnrollment,\n    27→    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    28→    AICertsSyncLog,\n    29→    AICertsSSOSession\n    30→)\n    31→from .services import (\n    32→    SSOService,\n    33→    EnrollmentSyncService,\n    34→    InstructorValidationService,\n    35→    AICERTsAPIError,\n    36→    AICERTsEnrollmentError\n    37→)\n    38→from .serializers import (\n    39→    AICertsEnrollmentSerializer,\n    40→    AICertsInstructorDesignationSerializer,\n    41→    AICertsSyncLogSerializer,\n    42→    EnrollUserSerializer,\n    43→    GenerateSSOSerializer\n    44→)\n    45→from apps.aicerts_courses.models import AiCertsCourse\n    46→\n    47→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    48→\n    49→\n    50→class AICertsEnrollmentViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n    51→    \"\"\"\n    52→    API endpoint for AICERTs course enrollments.\n    53→\n    54→    Features:\n    55→    - List user's enrollments\n    56→    - Create new enrollment (with auto-sync to AICERTs)\n    57→    - Check enrollment status\n    58→    - Retry failed enrollments\n    59→    \"\"\"\n    60→\n    61→    serializer_class = AICertsEnrollmentSerializer\n    62→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n    63→\n    64→    def get_queryset(self):\n    65→        \"\"\"Users can only see their own enrollments\"\"\"\n    66→        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n    67→            return AICertsEnrollment.objects.all()\n    68→        return AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n    69→\n    70→    @action(detail=False, methods=['post'], url_path='enroll')\n    71→    def enroll_user(self, request):\n    72→        \"\"\"\n    73→        Enroll authenticated user in an AICERTs course.\n    74→\n    75→        Request body:\n    76→        {\n    77→            \"course_id\": 123\n    78→        }\n    79→\n    80→        Response:\n    81→        {\n    82→            \"success\": true,\n    83→            \"enrollment_id\": 456,\n    84→            \"message\": \"Successfully enrolled in course\",\n    85→            \"sso_url\": \"https://learn.aicerts.io/...\"\n    86→        }\n    87→        \"\"\"\n    88→        serializer = EnrollUserSerializer(data=request.data)\n    89→        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n    90→\n    91→        course_id = serializer.validated_data['course_id']\n    92→        course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n    93→\n    94→        try:\n    95→            # Use enrollment sync service for dual enrollment\n    96→            enrollment, aicerts_result = EnrollmentSyncService.enroll_user_in_course(\n    97→                user=request.user,\n    98→                course=course,\n    99→                create_aicerts_user=settings.AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS\n   100→            )\n   101→\n   102→            # Generate SSO URL for immediate course access\n   103→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   104→                email=request.user.email,\n   105→                course_id=course_id\n   106→            )\n   107→\n   108→            # Log successful enrollment\n   109→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   110→                operation_type='user_enroll',\n   111→                status='success',\n   112→                user=request.user,\n   113→                course=course,\n   114→                response_data=aicerts_result\n   115→            )\n   116→\n   117→            return Response({\n   118→                'success': True,\n   119→                'enrollment_id': enrollment.id,\n   120→                'message': 'Successfully enrolled in course',\n   121→                'sso_url': sso_url,\n   122→                'already_enrolled': enrollment.aicerts_already_enrolled\n   123→            }, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)\n   124→\n   125→        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n   126→            logger.error(f\"Enrollment failed for user {request.user.email} in course {course_id}: {e}\")\n   127→\n   128→            # Log failed enrollment\n   129→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   130→                operation_type='user_enroll',\n   131→                status='failed',\n   132→                user=request.user,\n   133→                course=course,\n   134→                error_message=str(e)\n   135→            )\n   136→\n   137→            return Response({\n   138→                'success': False,\n   139→                'error': 'Enrollment failed',\n   140→                'details': str(e)\n   141→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   142→\n   143→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'], url_path='retry-sync')\n   144→    def retry_sync(self, request, pk=None):\n   145→        \"\"\"\n   146→        Retry failed enrollment sync with AICERTs.\n   147→\n   148→        Only works for enrollments with status='failed' and < 3 attempts.\n   149→        \"\"\"\n   150→        enrollment = self.get_object()\n   151→\n   152→        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'failed':\n   153→            return Response({\n   154→                'success': False,\n   155→                'error': 'Can only retry failed enrollments'\n   156→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   157→\n   158→        if enrollment.sync_attempts >= 3:\n   159→            return Response({\n   160→                'success': False,\n   161→                'error': 'Maximum retry attempts exceeded'\n   162→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   163→\n   164→        try:\n   165→            # Retry enrollment\n   166→            aicerts_result = SSOService.enroll_user(\n   167→                aicerts_user_id=enrollment.user.aicerts_user_id,\n   168→                course_id=enrollment.course.id,\n   169→                email=enrollment.user.email\n   170→            )\n   171→\n   172→            enrollment.mark_synced()\n   173→\n   174→            return Response({\n   175→                'success': True,\n   176→                'message': 'Enrollment synced successfully'\n   177→            })\n   178→\n   179→        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n   180→            enrollment.mark_failed(str(e))\n   181→\n   182→            return Response({\n   183→                'success': False,\n   184→                'error': 'Retry failed',\n   185→                'details': str(e)\n   186→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   187→\n   188→    @action(detail=True, methods=['get'], url_path='sso-url')\n   189→    def get_sso_url(self, request, pk=None):\n   190→        \"\"\"\n   191→        Generate SSO URL for this specific enrollment.\n   192→        \"\"\"\n   193→        enrollment = self.get_object()\n   194→\n   195→        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'enrolled':\n   196→            return Response({\n   197→                'success': False,\n   198→                'error': 'User not enrolled in this course'\n   199→            }, status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)\n   200→\n   201→        try:\n   202→            # Use external_id (AICERTS/Moodle course ID), not the Django PK\n   203→            aicerts_course_id = enrollment.course.external_id or enrollment.course.id\n   204→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   205→                email=enrollment.user.email,\n   206→                course_id=aicerts_course_id\n   207→            )\n   208→            return Response({\n   209→                'success': True,\n   210→                'sso_url': sso_url\n   211→            })\n   212→        except Exception as e:\n   213→            return Response({\n   214→                'success': False,\n   215→                'error': 'Failed to generate SSO URL',\n   216→                'details': str(e)\n   217→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   218→\n   219→\n   220→class SSORedirectView(APIView):\n   221→    \"\"\"\n   222→    Generate SSO URL and redirect user to AICERTs LMS for course access.\n   223→\n   224→    GET /api/aicerts/sso/redirect/?course_id=123\n   225→\n   226→    Redirects to AICERTs LMS with authenticated session.\n   227→    \"\"\"\n   228→\n   229→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n   230→\n   231→    def get(self, request):\n   232→        \"\"\"Generate SSO URL and redirect\"\"\"\n   233→        serializer = GenerateSSOSerializer(data=request.query_params)\n   234→        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n   235→\n   236→        course_id = serializer.validated_data.get('course_id')\n   237→        user_id = serializer.validated_data.get('user_id')\n   238→        \n   239→        # Staff can generate SSO for other users (for support/testing)\n   240→        if user_id and request.user.is_staff:\n   241→            target_user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n   242→        else:\n   243→            target_user = request.user\n   244→\n   245→        # Check if user is synced with AICERTs\n   246→        if not target_user.is_synced_with_aicerts():\n   247→            return JsonResponse({\n   248→                'success': False,\n   249→                'error': 'User account not synced with AICERTs',\n   250→                'message': f'User {target_user.email} not synced with AICERTs'\n   251→            }, status=400)\n   252→\n   253→        # Check if user is enrolled (if course_id provided)\n   254→        if course_id:\n   255→            enrollment = AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(\n   256→                user=target_user,\n   257→                course_id=course_id,\n   258→                aicerts_enrollment_status='enrolled'\n   259→            ).first()\n   260→\n   261→            if not enrollment:\n   262→                return JsonResponse({\n   263→                    'success': False,\n   264→                    'error': 'Not enrolled in this course',\n   265→                    'message': 'Please enroll in the course first'\n   266→                }, status=403)\n   267→\n   268→        try:\n   269→            # Generate SSO URL\n   270→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   271→                email=target_user.email,\n   272→                course_id=course_id\n   273→            )\n   274→\n   275→            # Create SSO session record\n   276→            course = None\n   277→            if course_id:\n   278→                course = AiCertsCourse.objects.filter(id=course_id).first()\n   279→\n   280→            sso_session = AICertsSSOSession.objects.create(\n   281→                user=target_user,\n   282→                course=course,\n   283→                sso_url=sso_url[:500],  # Truncate for DB storage\n   284→                session_token=secrets.token_urlsafe(32),\n   285→                ip_address=request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR'),\n   286→                user_agent=request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT', '')[:500],\n   287→                expires_at=timezone.now() + timedelta(hours=1)\n   288→            )\n   289→\n   290→            # Log SSO generation\n   291→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   292→                operation_type='user_auth',\n   293→                status='success',\n   294→                user=target_user,\n   295→                course=course,\n   296→                response_data={'session_token': sso_session.session_token, 'impersonated_by': request.user.id if user_id else None}\n   297→            )\n   298→\n   299→            # Redirect to AICERTs\n   300→            return redirect(sso_url)\n   301→\n   302→        except Exception as e:\n   303→            logger.error(f\"SSO generation failed for user {target_user.email}: {e}\")\n   304→\n   305→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   306→                operation_type='user_auth',\n   307→                status='failed',\n   308→                user=target_user,\n   309→                error_message=str(e)\n   310→            )\n   311→\n   312→            return JsonResponse({\n   313→                'success': False,\n   314→                'error': 'SSO generation failed',\n   315→                'details': str(e)\n   316→            }, status=500)\n   317→\n   318→\n   319→class InstructorValidationView(APIView):\n   320→    \"\"\"\n   321→    Validate instructor eligibility for AICERTs courses.\n   322→\n   323→    GET /api/aicerts/instructor/validate/?user_id=123&course_id=456\n   324→\n   325→    Returns:\n   326→    {\n   327→        \"is_aicerts_instructor\": true,\n   328→        \"can_instruct_course\": true,\n   329→        \"designated_courses\": [...]\n   330→    }\n   331→    \"\"\"\n   332→\n   333→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n   334→\n   335→    def get(self, request):\n   336→        \"\"\"Check if user can instruct specific course\"\"\"\n   337→        user_id = request.query_params.get('user_id')\n   338→        course_id = request.query_params.get('course_id')\n   339→\n   340→        if not user_id:\n   341→            return Response({\n   342→                'error': 'user_id required'\n   343→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   344→\n   345→        from apps.users.models import User\n   346→        user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n   347→\n   348→        response_data = {\n   349→            'user_id': user.id,\n   350→            'email': user.email,\n   351→            'is_aicerts_instructor': user.is_aicerts_instructor,\n   352→            'can_instruct_course': False,\n   353→            'designated_courses': []\n   354→        }\n   355→\n   356→        if user.is_aicerts_instructor:\n   357→            # Get designated courses\n   358→            designated_courses = InstructorValidationService.get_instructor_courses(user)\n   359→            response_data['designated_courses'] = [\n   360→                {\n   361→                    'id': course.id,\n   362→                    'title': course.title,\n   363→                    'shortname': course.shortname\n   364→                }\n   365→                for course in designated_courses\n   366→            ]\n   367→\n   368→            # Check specific course if provided\n   369→            if course_id:\n   370→                course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n   371→                response_data['can_instruct_course'] = InstructorValidationService.can_instruct_course(user, course)\n   372→                response_data['course'] = {\n   373→                    'id': course.id,\n   374→                    'title': course.title\n   375→                }\n   376→\n   377→        return Response(response_data)\n   378→\n   379→\n   380→class AICertsInstructorDesignationViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n   381→    \"\"\"\n   382→    API endpoint for managing instructor designations.\n   383→\n   384→    Only admins can create/update/delete designations.\n   385→    \"\"\"\n   386→\n   387→    serializer_class = AICertsInstructorDesignationSerializer\n   388→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n   389→    queryset = AICertsInstructorDesignation.objects.all()\n   390→\n   391→    def perform_create(self, serializer):\n   392→        \"\"\"Record who made the designation\"\"\"\n   393→        serializer.save(designated_by=self.request.user)\n   394→\n   395→\n   396→class AICertsSyncLogViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   397→    \"\"\"\n   398→    API endpoint for viewing sync logs (read-only).\n   399→\n   400→    Admins can see all logs, users can see their own.\n   401→    \"\"\"\n   402→\n   403→    serializer_class = AICertsSyncLogSerializer\n   404→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n   405→\n   406→    def get_queryset(self):\n   407→        \"\"\"Filter logs based on user permissions\"\"\"\n   408→        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n   409→            return AICertsSyncLog.objects.all()\n   410→        return AICertsSyncLog.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n   411→\n   412→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'], url_path='stats')\n   413→    def get_stats(self, request):\n   414→        \"\"\"\n   415→        Get sync statistics.\n   416→\n   417→        Returns counts by operation type and status.\n   418→        \"\"\"\n   419→        from django.db.models import Count\n   420→\n   421→        stats = AICertsSyncLog.objects.values('operation_type', 'status').annotate(\n   422→            count=Count('id')\n   423→        ).order_by('operation_type', 'status')\n   424→\n   425→        return Response({\n   426→            'stats': list(stats)\n   427→        })\n   428→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.
