{"parentUuid":"534bbb9e-caf2-418d-862f-05a3110dd0b7","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UfmCFpoRzVFGT2AcVKErUZ","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report.\n\n## Student Portal Exploration Report\n\nBased on my thorough analysis of the frontend and backend codebases, here's a complete understanding of the student portal architecture and implementation:\n\n---\n\n### 1. MAIN STUDENT PORTAL PAGE\n\n**File:** `/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/presentation/pages/student_portal/student_portal_page.dart` (832 lines)\n\n**Key Structure:**\n- **Widget:** `StudentPortalPage` - StatefulWidget that manages the entire portal interface\n- **Navigation:** 4-tab system with dynamic page switching via `_currentIndex`\n  - Tab 0: Dashboard\n  - Tab 1: My Courses\n  - Tab 2: Wishlist\n  - Tab 3: Progress\n\n**Layout Design:**\n- **Desktop (>800px):** \n  - Header with DashboardHeader (userName, notifications, cart, wishlist buttons)\n  - Horizontal navigation bar below header (Dashboard, My Courses, Wishlist, Progress icons)\n  - Content area constrained to max 1600px width\n  \n- **Mobile (<800px):**\n  - AppBar with title\n  - Bottom NavigationBar for tab switching\n  - Full-width content\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Uses `DashboardHeader` widget (shared with instructor dashboard)\n- Color scheme from `theme.colorScheme` (primary, surface, surfaceContainerLowest)\n- Responsive breakpoint at 800px width\n\n---\n\n### 2. \"MY COURSES\" SECTION - ENROLLED COURSES DISPLAY\n\n**Method:** `_buildMyCoursesView()` (lines 582-670)\n\n**Data Source:**\n```dart\nList<AICertsEnrollment> _enrollments = [];  // Fetched from backend API\n```\n\n**Initialization Flow:**\n1. `initState()` calls `_fetchEnrollments()`\n2. `_fetchEnrollments()` uses: `ApiClient.getAICertsEnrollments()`\n3. Response mapped to list of `AICertsEnrollment` objects\n\n**Empty State:**\n- Displays centered icon, message \"Your learning materials will appear here\"\n- \"Go to Learning Dashboard\" button redirects to `/courses`\n\n**Enrolled Courses Display:**\n- Title: \"My Enrolled Courses\" with refresh button\n- **ListView.separated** showing each enrollment as `_buildEnrollmentCard()`\n- If loading: shows `CircularProgressIndicator`\n\n**Enrollment Card (`_buildEnrollmentCard()`)** - Lines 672-782:\n- **Layout:**\n  - 80x80 course icon (school icon in circle)\n  - Course title (bold)\n  - Status badge (green \"ENROLLED\" or orange status)\n  - Enrollment date: \"Enrolled: DD/MM/YYYY\"\n  - Progress bar (LinearProgressIndicator with min height 6)\n  - Progress percentage on right (\"65%\")\n  - \"Launch Course\" button\n  \n- **Properties Displayed:**\n  - `enrollment.courseTitle`\n  - `enrollment.status`\n  - `enrollment.enrolledAt` (date parsing)\n  - `enrollment.progress` (0-100 scale)\n  \n- **Interactivity:**\n  - Launch button calls `_launchCourse(enrollment)` for enrolled status\n  - Button disabled if status != 'enrolled'\n\n---\n\n### 3. QUICK STATS / DASHBOARD VIEW\n\n**Method:** `_buildDashboardView()` (lines 292-419)\n\n**Layout Sections:**\n\n**A. Welcome Header (lines 302-326):**\n```\n\"Welcome back, [userName]!\"\n\"Here is an overview of your learning journey\"\n```\n- Right side: notification badge with unread count\n\n**B. Overview Stats Grid (lines 329-374):**\n- Responsive 3-column layout (1 column on small screens)\n- Cards with icon, label, value, and color-coded badge\n\n**Three Stat Cards:**\n1. **Enrolled Courses** \n   - Icon: `Icons.school_rounded`\n   - Value: \"4\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `colors.primary`\n\n2. **Certifications**\n   - Icon: `Icons.verified_rounded`\n   - Value: \"1\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `Color(0xFF10B981)` (successGreen)\n\n3. **Learning Hours**\n   - Icon: `Icons.auto_graph_rounded`\n   - Value: \"12.5h\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `colors.tertiary`\n\n**Stat Card Component (`_buildStatCard()`)** - Lines 527-580:\n- Container with rounded corners (20px)\n- Background: `colors.surface` with border and shadow\n- Icon in 12x12 colored circle\n- Large headline value\n- Small label text\n\n**C. Recent Course Activity (lines 377-416):**\n- Title with \"View All\" button\n- 2 hardcoded activity cards showing course progress and completion\n\n**Activity Card Component** - Lines 454-525:\n- Icon + title + status bar layout\n- Status: \"In Progress • 65%\" or \"Completed\"\n- Timestamp: \"2 hours ago\", \"Yesterday\"\n- Chevron icon on right\n\n---\n\n### 4. AICERTS ENROLLMENT DATA FETCHING\n\n**Frontend Flow:**\n\n**Step 1: API Call (`api_client.dart` lines 287-302)**\n```dart\nstatic Future<List<Map<String, dynamic>>> getAICertsEnrollments() async {\n  final response = await get('/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/');\n  // Handles both List and paginated response.data['results']\n  return List<Map<String, dynamic>>.from(response.data);\n}\n\nstatic Future<String?> getAICertsSSOUrl(int enrollmentId) async {\n  final response = await get('/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/$enrollmentId/sso-url/');\n  return response.data['sso_url'] as String?;\n}\n```\n\n**Step 2: Model Mapping (`aicerts_enrollment.dart`)**\n\n**AICertsEnrollment Model:**\n```dart\nclass AICertsEnrollment {\n  final int id;\n  final int courseId;\n  final String courseTitle;\n  final String? courseShortname;\n  final String status;\n  final DateTime enrolledAt;\n  final double progress;\n  final DateTime? lastAccessedAt;\n  final DateTime? completedAt;\n  \n  factory AICertsEnrollment.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) {\n    return AICertsEnrollment(\n      id: json['id'] as int,\n      courseId: json['course'] as int,\n      courseTitle: json['course_title'] ?? 'Unknown Course',\n      courseShortname: json['course_shortname'],\n      status: json['aicerts_enrollment_status'] ?? 'pending',\n      enrolledAt: DateTime.parse(json['enrolled_at']),\n      progress: (json['progress_percentage'] as num?)?.toDouble() ?? 0.0,\n      lastAccessedAt: json['last_accessed_at'] != null ? DateTime.parse(...) : null,\n      completedAt: json['completed_at'] != null ? DateTime.parse(...) : null,\n    );\n  }\n  \n  bool get isEnrolled => status == 'enrolled';\n}\n```\n\n**Step 3: State Management:**\n- Fetched in `initState()` via `_fetchEnrollments()`\n- Stored in `_enrollments` list\n- Re-renders \"My Courses\" page with `setState()`\n- Manual refresh via refresh button calls `_fetchEnrollments()` again\n\n---\n\n### 5. BACKEND API ENDPOINTS\n\n#### **A. AICerts Integration Endpoints**\n**Base:** `/api/v1/aicerts/`\n\n**Endpoints:**\n| Method | Path | Purpose | Auth |\n|--------|------|---------|------|\n| GET | `enrollments/` | List user's enrollments | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `enrollments/enroll/` | Enroll user in course | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `enrollments/{id}/sso-url/` | Get SSO URL for enrollment | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `enrollments/{id}/retry-sync/` | Retry failed sync | IsAuthenticated |\n\n**ViewSet:** `AICertsEnrollmentViewSet` (`views.py` lines 50-217)\n\n**Key Methods:**\n\n1. **List Enrollments (default list action):**\n   - Filters by `user=request.user` (unless staff → see all)\n   - Returns serialized `AICertsEnrollment` objects\n   - Serializer: `AICertsEnrollmentSerializer` (lines 17-64)\n\n2. **Get SSO URL (`get_sso_url`)** - lines 188-217:\n   - Validates `aicerts_enrollment_status == 'enrolled'`\n   - Uses `course.external_id` (AICERTS Moodle ID) or falls back to `course.id`\n   - Calls `SSOService.generate_sso_url(email, course_id)`\n   - Returns JSON with `sso_url`\n\n#### **B. Learner Portal Endpoints**\n**Base:** `/api/v1/learner_portal/`\n\n**Endpoints:**\n| Method | Path | Purpose | Auth |\n|--------|------|---------|------|\n| GET | `profile/me/` | Get current user profile | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `profile/update_company_details/` | Update company info | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `profile/check_existing_student/` | Check enrollment history | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `wishlist/` | List user's wishlist | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `wishlist/{id}/move_to_cart/` | Move wishlist to cart | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `cart/active/` | Get active shopping cart | Any |\n| POST | `cart/{id}/add_item/` | Add course to cart | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `catalog/` | List course catalog | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `catalog/{id}/add_to_wishlist/` | Add catalog item to wishlist | IsAuthenticated |\n\n---\n\n### 6. BACKEND MODELS & SERIALIZERS\n\n#### **AICerts Models** (`aicerts_integration/models.py`)\n\n**AICertsEnrollment:**\n- Tracks enrollment sync between HOSI and AICERTS\n- Fields:\n  - `user` (FK to User)\n  - `course` (FK to AiCertsCourse)\n  - `aicerts_enrollment_status` (enrolled, failed, pending, etc.)\n  - `aicerts_already_enrolled` (boolean - if already existed on AICERTS)\n  - `enrolled_at`, `synced_at`, `completed_at`\n  - `progress_percentage`, `sync_attempts`, `sync_error`\n  - `certificate_issued_at`\n\n**AICertsEnrollmentSerializer:**\n- Read-only fields: `user_email`, `course_title`, `course_shortname`, `status`, `progress_percentage`, dates\n- Maps JSON response to frontend model\n\n#### **Learner Portal Models** (`learner_portal/models.py`)\n\n**1. StudentProfile** (lines 10-146):\n- Extended student data\n- Stores company payment history (reuse)\n- Location preferences (country, state, city)\n- Payment preferences (provider, method)\n- Enrollment statistics (total, active, completed)\n\n**2. Wishlist** (lines 148-320):\n- Generic FK to any course type (masterclass, learnership, industry, custom)\n- Tracks interest level, intended start time, notes\n- Marketing contact tracking for leads\n- Conversion tracking (to cart, to enrollment)\n- Indexes on user+training_type, marketing_contacted, conversion tracking\n\n**3. CourseCart** (lines 322-461):\n- Shopping cart per user\n- Status: active, checkout, completed, abandoned\n- Corporate enrollment flag\n- Reuse company details flag\n- Totals: total_courses, total_amount, currency\n- Timestamps: checkout_started_at, completed_at, abandoned_at\n\n**4. CourseCartItem** (lines 463-568):\n- Individual cart items\n- Generic FK to course + training type\n- Price and currency\n- Prerequisites met tracking\n- Added_from_wishlist flag\n- Auto-recalculates cart totals on save\n\n**5. CourseCatalogItem** (lines 654-769):\n- Unified catalog entry for all course types\n- Denormalized: title, description, thumbnail, price\n- Provider FK\n- Status: active, featured\n- Stats: total_enrollments, total_wishlist_adds\n- Unique constraint: one entry per content_type+object_id\n\n---\n\n### 7. ENHANCED STUDENT DASHBOARD (Alternative Portal)\n\n**File:** `dashboard/enhanced_student_dashboard.dart` (61.3KB, alternative dashboard)\n\n**Features:**\n- Subscription status tracking (active, grace_period, suspended, cancelled)\n- 5-day grace period enforcement\n- Paywall enforcement for suspended learners\n- Can access: community chat only (no courses if suspended)\n- Multi-enrollment support\n\n**This appears to be a more business-focused version with subscription/payment logic**\n\n---\n\n### 8. KEY ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS\n\n**Frontend:**\n1. **State Management:** `StatefulWidget` with `setState()`\n2. **Data Fetching:** `ApiClient.get()` with Dio HTTP client\n3. **Model Mapping:** `.fromJson()` factory constructors\n4. **Responsive Design:** LayoutBuilder + MediaQuery breakpoints (800px)\n5. **Error Handling:** Try-catch with snackbars\n\n**Backend:**\n1. **Serialization:** DRF serializers with computed fields\n2. **Content Types:** Generic FK for multi-type course support\n3. **Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`, `IsAdminUser`, `AllowAny` decorators\n4. **Filtering:** User-specific queryset filtering\n5. **Localization:** CurrencyService, LocalizedPriceField, CascadingDropdowns (Country→State→City)\n\n---\n\n### 9. QUICK REFERENCE: API ENDPOINTS FOR STUDENT PORTAL\n\n**Get My AICERTS Enrollments:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Launch Course (Get SSO URL):**\n```\nGET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/{enrollment_id}/sso-url/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Get Cart:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/cart/active/\n```\n\n**Get Wishlist:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/wishlist/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Get Catalog:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/catalog/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\nThe student portal is a comprehensive learning management interface with:\n- **Multi-tab navigation** (Dashboard, Courses, Wishlist, Progress)\n- **Real-time enrollment tracking** pulling from AICERTS API\n- **Smart shopping cart** with drag-drop from catalog/wishlist\n- **Lead tracking** via wishlist + conversion analytics\n- **Generic course system** supporting 4 training types\n- **Responsive design** adapting to mobile/tablet/desktop\n- **Payment history tracking** for quick reuse on repeat purchases\n\nAll files are production-ready with proper error handling, validation, and authentication checks."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a50a75431ef7ec792 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 81466\ntool_uses: 26\nduration_ms: 62459</usage>"}]}]},"uuid":"78cff8c8-0914-41d4-9b2e-c273c4cf93dc","timestamp":"2026-03-01T11:14:33.064Z","toolUseResult":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Explore the student portal in /home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/presentation/pages/ and /home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/learner_portal/. Read the key files fully.","agentId":"a50a75431ef7ec792","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report.\n\n## Student Portal Exploration Report\n\nBased on my thorough analysis of the frontend and backend codebases, here's a complete understanding of the student portal architecture and implementation:\n\n---\n\n### 1. MAIN STUDENT PORTAL PAGE\n\n**File:** `/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/presentation/pages/student_portal/student_portal_page.dart` (832 lines)\n\n**Key Structure:**\n- **Widget:** `StudentPortalPage` - StatefulWidget that manages the entire portal interface\n- **Navigation:** 4-tab system with dynamic page switching via `_currentIndex`\n  - Tab 0: Dashboard\n  - Tab 1: My Courses\n  - Tab 2: Wishlist\n  - Tab 3: Progress\n\n**Layout Design:**\n- **Desktop (>800px):** \n  - Header with DashboardHeader (userName, notifications, cart, wishlist buttons)\n  - Horizontal navigation bar below header (Dashboard, My Courses, Wishlist, Progress icons)\n  - Content area constrained to max 1600px width\n  \n- **Mobile (<800px):**\n  - AppBar with title\n  - Bottom NavigationBar for tab switching\n  - Full-width content\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Uses `DashboardHeader` widget (shared with instructor dashboard)\n- Color scheme from `theme.colorScheme` (primary, surface, surfaceContainerLowest)\n- Responsive breakpoint at 800px width\n\n---\n\n### 2. \"MY COURSES\" SECTION - ENROLLED COURSES DISPLAY\n\n**Method:** `_buildMyCoursesView()` (lines 582-670)\n\n**Data Source:**\n```dart\nList<AICertsEnrollment> _enrollments = [];  // Fetched from backend API\n```\n\n**Initialization Flow:**\n1. `initState()` calls `_fetchEnrollments()`\n2. `_fetchEnrollments()` uses: `ApiClient.getAICertsEnrollments()`\n3. Response mapped to list of `AICertsEnrollment` objects\n\n**Empty State:**\n- Displays centered icon, message \"Your learning materials will appear here\"\n- \"Go to Learning Dashboard\" button redirects to `/courses`\n\n**Enrolled Courses Display:**\n- Title: \"My Enrolled Courses\" with refresh button\n- **ListView.separated** showing each enrollment as `_buildEnrollmentCard()`\n- If loading: shows `CircularProgressIndicator`\n\n**Enrollment Card (`_buildEnrollmentCard()`)** - Lines 672-782:\n- **Layout:**\n  - 80x80 course icon (school icon in circle)\n  - Course title (bold)\n  - Status badge (green \"ENROLLED\" or orange status)\n  - Enrollment date: \"Enrolled: DD/MM/YYYY\"\n  - Progress bar (LinearProgressIndicator with min height 6)\n  - Progress percentage on right (\"65%\")\n  - \"Launch Course\" button\n  \n- **Properties Displayed:**\n  - `enrollment.courseTitle`\n  - `enrollment.status`\n  - `enrollment.enrolledAt` (date parsing)\n  - `enrollment.progress` (0-100 scale)\n  \n- **Interactivity:**\n  - Launch button calls `_launchCourse(enrollment)` for enrolled status\n  - Button disabled if status != 'enrolled'\n\n---\n\n### 3. QUICK STATS / DASHBOARD VIEW\n\n**Method:** `_buildDashboardView()` (lines 292-419)\n\n**Layout Sections:**\n\n**A. Welcome Header (lines 302-326):**\n```\n\"Welcome back, [userName]!\"\n\"Here is an overview of your learning journey\"\n```\n- Right side: notification badge with unread count\n\n**B. Overview Stats Grid (lines 329-374):**\n- Responsive 3-column layout (1 column on small screens)\n- Cards with icon, label, value, and color-coded badge\n\n**Three Stat Cards:**\n1. **Enrolled Courses** \n   - Icon: `Icons.school_rounded`\n   - Value: \"4\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `colors.primary`\n\n2. **Certifications**\n   - Icon: `Icons.verified_rounded`\n   - Value: \"1\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `Color(0xFF10B981)` (successGreen)\n\n3. **Learning Hours**\n   - Icon: `Icons.auto_graph_rounded`\n   - Value: \"12.5h\" (hardcoded)\n   - Color: `colors.tertiary`\n\n**Stat Card Component (`_buildStatCard()`)** - Lines 527-580:\n- Container with rounded corners (20px)\n- Background: `colors.surface` with border and shadow\n- Icon in 12x12 colored circle\n- Large headline value\n- Small label text\n\n**C. Recent Course Activity (lines 377-416):**\n- Title with \"View All\" button\n- 2 hardcoded activity cards showing course progress and completion\n\n**Activity Card Component** - Lines 454-525:\n- Icon + title + status bar layout\n- Status: \"In Progress • 65%\" or \"Completed\"\n- Timestamp: \"2 hours ago\", \"Yesterday\"\n- Chevron icon on right\n\n---\n\n### 4. AICERTS ENROLLMENT DATA FETCHING\n\n**Frontend Flow:**\n\n**Step 1: API Call (`api_client.dart` lines 287-302)**\n```dart\nstatic Future<List<Map<String, dynamic>>> getAICertsEnrollments() async {\n  final response = await get('/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/');\n  // Handles both List and paginated response.data['results']\n  return List<Map<String, dynamic>>.from(response.data);\n}\n\nstatic Future<String?> getAICertsSSOUrl(int enrollmentId) async {\n  final response = await get('/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/$enrollmentId/sso-url/');\n  return response.data['sso_url'] as String?;\n}\n```\n\n**Step 2: Model Mapping (`aicerts_enrollment.dart`)**\n\n**AICertsEnrollment Model:**\n```dart\nclass AICertsEnrollment {\n  final int id;\n  final int courseId;\n  final String courseTitle;\n  final String? courseShortname;\n  final String status;\n  final DateTime enrolledAt;\n  final double progress;\n  final DateTime? lastAccessedAt;\n  final DateTime? completedAt;\n  \n  factory AICertsEnrollment.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) {\n    return AICertsEnrollment(\n      id: json['id'] as int,\n      courseId: json['course'] as int,\n      courseTitle: json['course_title'] ?? 'Unknown Course',\n      courseShortname: json['course_shortname'],\n      status: json['aicerts_enrollment_status'] ?? 'pending',\n      enrolledAt: DateTime.parse(json['enrolled_at']),\n      progress: (json['progress_percentage'] as num?)?.toDouble() ?? 0.0,\n      lastAccessedAt: json['last_accessed_at'] != null ? DateTime.parse(...) : null,\n      completedAt: json['completed_at'] != null ? DateTime.parse(...) : null,\n    );\n  }\n  \n  bool get isEnrolled => status == 'enrolled';\n}\n```\n\n**Step 3: State Management:**\n- Fetched in `initState()` via `_fetchEnrollments()`\n- Stored in `_enrollments` list\n- Re-renders \"My Courses\" page with `setState()`\n- Manual refresh via refresh button calls `_fetchEnrollments()` again\n\n---\n\n### 5. BACKEND API ENDPOINTS\n\n#### **A. AICerts Integration Endpoints**\n**Base:** `/api/v1/aicerts/`\n\n**Endpoints:**\n| Method | Path | Purpose | Auth |\n|--------|------|---------|------|\n| GET | `enrollments/` | List user's enrollments | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `enrollments/enroll/` | Enroll user in course | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `enrollments/{id}/sso-url/` | Get SSO URL for enrollment | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `enrollments/{id}/retry-sync/` | Retry failed sync | IsAuthenticated |\n\n**ViewSet:** `AICertsEnrollmentViewSet` (`views.py` lines 50-217)\n\n**Key Methods:**\n\n1. **List Enrollments (default list action):**\n   - Filters by `user=request.user` (unless staff → see all)\n   - Returns serialized `AICertsEnrollment` objects\n   - Serializer: `AICertsEnrollmentSerializer` (lines 17-64)\n\n2. **Get SSO URL (`get_sso_url`)** - lines 188-217:\n   - Validates `aicerts_enrollment_status == 'enrolled'`\n   - Uses `course.external_id` (AICERTS Moodle ID) or falls back to `course.id`\n   - Calls `SSOService.generate_sso_url(email, course_id)`\n   - Returns JSON with `sso_url`\n\n#### **B. Learner Portal Endpoints**\n**Base:** `/api/v1/learner_portal/`\n\n**Endpoints:**\n| Method | Path | Purpose | Auth |\n|--------|------|---------|------|\n| GET | `profile/me/` | Get current user profile | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `profile/update_company_details/` | Update company info | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `profile/check_existing_student/` | Check enrollment history | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `wishlist/` | List user's wishlist | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `wishlist/{id}/move_to_cart/` | Move wishlist to cart | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `cart/active/` | Get active shopping cart | Any |\n| POST | `cart/{id}/add_item/` | Add course to cart | IsAuthenticated |\n| GET | `catalog/` | List course catalog | IsAuthenticated |\n| POST | `catalog/{id}/add_to_wishlist/` | Add catalog item to wishlist | IsAuthenticated |\n\n---\n\n### 6. BACKEND MODELS & SERIALIZERS\n\n#### **AICerts Models** (`aicerts_integration/models.py`)\n\n**AICertsEnrollment:**\n- Tracks enrollment sync between HOSI and AICERTS\n- Fields:\n  - `user` (FK to User)\n  - `course` (FK to AiCertsCourse)\n  - `aicerts_enrollment_status` (enrolled, failed, pending, etc.)\n  - `aicerts_already_enrolled` (boolean - if already existed on AICERTS)\n  - `enrolled_at`, `synced_at`, `completed_at`\n  - `progress_percentage`, `sync_attempts`, `sync_error`\n  - `certificate_issued_at`\n\n**AICertsEnrollmentSerializer:**\n- Read-only fields: `user_email`, `course_title`, `course_shortname`, `status`, `progress_percentage`, dates\n- Maps JSON response to frontend model\n\n#### **Learner Portal Models** (`learner_portal/models.py`)\n\n**1. StudentProfile** (lines 10-146):\n- Extended student data\n- Stores company payment history (reuse)\n- Location preferences (country, state, city)\n- Payment preferences (provider, method)\n- Enrollment statistics (total, active, completed)\n\n**2. Wishlist** (lines 148-320):\n- Generic FK to any course type (masterclass, learnership, industry, custom)\n- Tracks interest level, intended start time, notes\n- Marketing contact tracking for leads\n- Conversion tracking (to cart, to enrollment)\n- Indexes on user+training_type, marketing_contacted, conversion tracking\n\n**3. CourseCart** (lines 322-461):\n- Shopping cart per user\n- Status: active, checkout, completed, abandoned\n- Corporate enrollment flag\n- Reuse company details flag\n- Totals: total_courses, total_amount, currency\n- Timestamps: checkout_started_at, completed_at, abandoned_at\n\n**4. CourseCartItem** (lines 463-568):\n- Individual cart items\n- Generic FK to course + training type\n- Price and currency\n- Prerequisites met tracking\n- Added_from_wishlist flag\n- Auto-recalculates cart totals on save\n\n**5. CourseCatalogItem** (lines 654-769):\n- Unified catalog entry for all course types\n- Denormalized: title, description, thumbnail, price\n- Provider FK\n- Status: active, featured\n- Stats: total_enrollments, total_wishlist_adds\n- Unique constraint: one entry per content_type+object_id\n\n---\n\n### 7. ENHANCED STUDENT DASHBOARD (Alternative Portal)\n\n**File:** `dashboard/enhanced_student_dashboard.dart` (61.3KB, alternative dashboard)\n\n**Features:**\n- Subscription status tracking (active, grace_period, suspended, cancelled)\n- 5-day grace period enforcement\n- Paywall enforcement for suspended learners\n- Can access: community chat only (no courses if suspended)\n- Multi-enrollment support\n\n**This appears to be a more business-focused version with subscription/payment logic**\n\n---\n\n### 8. KEY ARCHITECTURE PATTERNS\n\n**Frontend:**\n1. **State Management:** `StatefulWidget` with `setState()`\n2. **Data Fetching:** `ApiClient.get()` with Dio HTTP client\n3. **Model Mapping:** `.fromJson()` factory constructors\n4. **Responsive Design:** LayoutBuilder + MediaQuery breakpoints (800px)\n5. **Error Handling:** Try-catch with snackbars\n\n**Backend:**\n1. **Serialization:** DRF serializers with computed fields\n2. **Content Types:** Generic FK for multi-type course support\n3. **Permissions:** `IsAuthenticated`, `IsAdminUser`, `AllowAny` decorators\n4. **Filtering:** User-specific queryset filtering\n5. **Localization:** CurrencyService, LocalizedPriceField, CascadingDropdowns (Country→State→City)\n\n---\n\n### 9. QUICK REFERENCE: API ENDPOINTS FOR STUDENT PORTAL\n\n**Get My AICERTS Enrollments:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Launch Course (Get SSO URL):**\n```\nGET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/{enrollment_id}/sso-url/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Get Cart:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/cart/active/\n```\n\n**Get Wishlist:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/wishlist/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n**Get Catalog:**\n```\nGET /api/v1/learner_portal/catalog/\nAuthorization: Bearer {token}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\nThe student portal is a comprehensive learning management interface with:\n- **Multi-tab navigation** (Dashboard, Courses, Wishlist, Progress)\n- **Real-time enrollment tracking** pulling from AICERTS API\n- **Smart shopping cart** with drag-drop from catalog/wishlist\n- **Lead tracking** via wishlist + conversion analytics\n- **Generic course system** supporting 4 training types\n- **Responsive design** adapting to mobile/tablet/desktop\n- **Payment history tracking** for quick reuse on repeat purchases\n\nAll files are production-ready with proper error handling, validation, and authentication checks."}],"totalDurationMs":62459,"totalTokens":81466,"totalToolUseCount":26,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1215,"cache_read_input_tokens":76457,"output_tokens":3791,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1215},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"534bbb9e-caf2-418d-862f-05a3110dd0b7"}

import AiCertsCourse\n    46→\n    47→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    48→\n    49→\n    50→class AICertsEnrollmentViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n    51→    \"\"\"\n    52→    API endpoint for AICERTs course enrollments.\n    53→\n    54→    Features:\n    55→    - List user's enrollments\n    56→    - Create new enrollment (with auto-sync to AICERTs)\n    57→    - Check enrollment status\n    58→    - Retry failed enrollments\n    59→    \"\"\"\n    60→\n    61→    serializer_class = AICertsEnrollmentSerializer\n    62→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n    63→\n    64→    def get_queryset(self):\n    65→        \"\"\"Users can only see their own enrollments\"\"\"\n    66→        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n    67→            return AICertsEnrollment.objects.all()\n    68→        return AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n    69→\n    70→    @action(detail=False, methods=['post'], url_path='enroll')\n    71→    def enroll_user(self, request):\n    72→        \"\"\"\n    73→        Enroll authenticated user in an AICERTs course.\n    74→\n    75→        Request body:\n    76→        {\n    77→ EnrollUserSerializer(data=request.data)\n    89→        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n    90→\n    91→        course_id = serializer.validated_data['course_id']\n    92→        course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n    93→\n    94→        try:\n    95→            # Use enrollment sync service for dual enrollment\n    96→            enrollment, aicerts_result = EnrollmentSyncService.enroll_user_in_course(\n    97→                user=request.user,\n    98→                course=course,\n    99→                create_aicerts_user=settings.AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS\n   100→            )\n   101→\n   102→            # Generate SSO URL for immediate course access\n   103→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   104→                email=request.user.email,\n   105→                course_id=course_id\n   106→            )\n   107→\n   108→            # Log successful enrollment\n   109→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   110→                operation_type='user_enroll',\n   111→                status='success',\n   112→                user=request.user,\n   113→                course=course,\n   114→                response_data=aicerts_result\n   115→            )\n   116→\n   117→            return Response({\n   118→                'success': True,\n   119→                'enrollment_id': enrollment.id,\n   120→                'message': 'Successfully enrolled in course',\n   121→                'sso_url': sso_url,\n   122→                'already_enrolled': enrollment.aicerts_already_enrolled\n   123→            }, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)\n   124→\n   125→        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n   126→            logger.error(f\"Enrollment failed for user {request.user.email} in course {course_id}: {e}\")\n   127→\n   128→            # Log failed enrollment\n   129→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   130→                operation_type='user_enroll',\n   131→                status='failed',\n   132→                user=request.user,\n   133→                course=course,\n   134→                error_message=str(e)\n   135→            )\n   136→\n   137→            return Response({\n   138→                'success': False,\n   139→                'error': 'Enrollment failed',\n   140→                'details': str(e)\n   141→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   142→\n   143→    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'], url_path='retry-sync')\n   144→    def retry_sync(self, request, pk=None):\n   145→        \"\"\"\n   146→        Retry failed enrollment sync with AICERTs.\n   147→\n   148→        Only works for enrollments with status='failed' and < 3 attempts.\n   149→        \"\"\"\n   150→        enrollment = self.get_object()\n   151→\n   152→        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'failed':\n   153→            return Response({\n   154→                'success': False,\n   155→                'error': 'Can only retry failed enrollments'\n   156→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   157→\n   158→        if enrollment.sync_attempts >= 3:\n   159→            return Response({\n   160→                'success': False,\n   161→                'error': 'Maximum retry attempts exceeded'\n   162→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   163→\n   164→        try:\n   165→            # Retry enrollment\n   166→            aicerts_result = SSOService.enroll_user(\n   167→                aicerts_user_id=enrollment.user.aicerts_user_id,\n   168→                course_id=enrollment.course.id,\n   169→                email=enrollment.user.email\n   170→            )\n   171→\n   172→            enrollment.mark_synced()\n   173→\n   174→            return Response({\n   175→                'success': True,\n   176→                'message': 'Enrollment synced successfully'\n   177→            })\n   178→\n   179→        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n   180→            enrollment.mark_failed(str(e))\n   181→\n   182→            return Response({\n   183→                'success': False,\n   184→                'error': 'Retry failed',\n   185→                'details': str(e)\n   186→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   187→\n   188→    @action(detail=True, methods=['get'], url_path='sso-url')\n   189→    def get_sso_url(self, request, pk=None):\n   190→        \"\"\"\n   191→        Generate SSO URL for this specific enrollment.\n   192→        \"\"\"\n   193→        enrollment = self.get_object()\n   194→\n   195→        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'enrolled':\n   196→            return Response({\n   197→                'success': False,\n   198→                'error': 'User not enrolled in this course'\n   199→            }, status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)\n   200→\n   201→        try:\n   202→            # Use external_id (AICERTS/Moodle course ID), not the Django PK\n   203→            aicerts_course_id = enrollment.course.external_id or enrollment.course.id\n   204→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   205→                email=enrollment.user.email,\n   206→                course_id=aicerts_course_id\n   207→            )\n   208→            return Response({\n   209→                'success': True,\n   210→                'sso_url': sso_url\n   211→            })\n   212→        except Exception as e:\n   213→            return Response({\n   214→                'success': False,\n   215→                'error': 'Failed to generate SSO URL',\n   216→                'details': str(e)\n   217→            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n   218→\n   219→\n   220→class SSORedirectView(APIView):\n   221→    \"\"\"\n   222→    Generate SSO URL and redirect user to AICERTs LMS for course access.\n   223→\n   224→    GET /api/aicerts/sso/redirect/?course_id=123\n   225→\n   226→    Redirects to AICERTs LMS with authenticated session.\n   227→    \"\"\"\n   228→\n   229→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n   230→\n   231→    def get(self, request):\n   232→        \"\"\"Generate SSO URL and redirect\"\"\"\n   233→        serializer = GenerateSSOSerializer(data=request.query_params)\n   234→        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n   235→\n   236→        course_id = serializer.validated_data.get('course_id')\n   237→        user_id = serializer.validated_data.get('user_id')\n   238→        \n   239→        # Staff can generate SSO for other users (for support/testing)\n   240→        if user_id and request.user.is_staff:\n   241→            target_user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n   242→        else:\n   243→            target_user = request.user\n   244→\n   245→        # Check if user is synced with AICERTs\n   246→        if not target_user.is_synced_with_aicerts():\n   247→            return JsonResponse({\n   248→                'success': False,\n   249→                'error': 'User account not synced with AICERTs',\n   250→                'message': f'User {target_user.email} not synced with AICERTs'\n   251→            }, status=400)\n   252→\n   253→        # Check if user is enrolled (if course_id provided)\n   254→        if course_id:\n   255→            enrollment = AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(\n   256→                user=target_user,\n   257→                course_id=course_id,\n   258→                aicerts_enrollment_status='enrolled'\n   259→            ).first()\n   260→\n   261→            if not enrollment:\n   262→                return JsonResponse({\n   263→                    'success': False,\n   264→                    'error': 'Not enrolled in this course',\n   265→                    'message': 'Please enroll in the course first'\n   266→                }, status=403)\n   267→\n   268→        try:\n   269→            # Generate SSO URL\n   270→            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n   271→                email=target_user.email,\n   272→                course_id=course_id\n   273→            )\n   274→\n   275→            # Create SSO session record\n   276→            course = None\n   277→            if course_id:\n   278→                course = AiCertsCourse.objects.filter(id=course_id).first()\n   279→\n   280→            sso_session = AICertsSSOSession.objects.create(\n   281→                user=target_user,\n   282→                course=course,\n   283→                sso_url=sso_url[:500],  # Truncate for DB storage\n   284→                session_token=secrets.token_urlsafe(32),\n   285→                ip_address=request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR'),\n   286→                user_agent=request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT', '')[:500],\n   287→                expires_at=timezone.now() + timedelta(hours=1)\n   288→            )\n   289→\n   290→            # Log SSO generation\n   291→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   292→                operation_type='user_auth',\n   293→                status='success',\n   294→                user=target_user,\n   295→                course=course,\n   296→                response_data={'session_token': sso_session.session_token, 'impersonated_by': request.user.id if user_id else None}\n   297→            )\n   298→\n   299→            # Redirect to AICERTs\n   300→            return redirect(sso_url)\n   301→\n   302→        except Exception as e:\n   303→            logger.error(f\"SSO generation failed for user {target_user.email}: {e}\")\n   304→\n   305→            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n   306→                operation_type='user_auth',\n   307→                status='failed',\n   308→                user=target_user,\n   309→                error_message=str(e)\n   310→            )\n   311→\n   312→            return JsonResponse({\n   313→                'success': False,\n   314→                'error': 'SSO generation failed',\n   315→                'details': str(e)\n   316→            }, status=500)\n   317→\n   318→\n   319→class InstructorValidationView(APIView):\n   320→    \"\"\"\n   321→    Validate instructor eligibility for AICERTs courses.\n   322→\n   323→    GET /api/aicerts/instructor/validate/?user_id=123&course_id=456\n   324→\n   325→    Returns:\n   326→    {\n   327→        \"is_aicerts_instructor\": true,\n   328→        \"can_instruct_course\": true,\n   329→        \"designated_courses\": [...]\n   330→    }\n   331→    \"\"\"\n   332→\n   333→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n   334→\n   335→    def get(self, request):\n   336→        \"\"\"Check if user can instruct specific course\"\"\"\n   337→        user_id = request.query_params.get('user_id')\n   338→        course_id = request.query_params.get('course_id')\n   339→\n   340→        if not user_id:\n   341→            return Response({\n   342→                'error': 'user_id required'\n   343→            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n   344→\n   345→        from apps.users.models import User\n   346→        user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n   347→\n   348→        response_data = {\n   349→            'user_id': user.id,\n   350→            'email': user.email,\n   351→            'is_aicerts_instructor': user.is_aicerts_instructor,\n   352→            'can_instruct_course': False,\n   353→            'designated_courses': []\n   354→        }\n   355→\n   356→        if user.is_aicerts_instructor:\n   357→            # Get designated courses\n   358→            designated_courses = InstructorValidationService.get_instructor_courses(user)\n   359→            response_data['designated_courses'] = [\n   360→                {\n   361→                    'id': course.id,\n   362→                    'title': course.title,\n   363→                    'shortname': course.shortname\n   364→                }\n   365→                for course in designated_courses\n   366→            ]\n   367→\n   368→            # Check specific course if provided\n   369→            if course_id:\n   370→                course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n   371→                response_data['can_instruct_course'] = InstructorValidationService.can_instruct_course(user, course)\n   372→                response_data['course'] = {\n   373→                    'id': course.id,\n   374→                    'title': course.title\n   375→                }\n   376→\n   377→        return Response(response_data)\n   378→\n   379→\n   380→class AICertsInstructorDesignationViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n   381→    \"\"\"\n   382→    API endpoint for managing instructor designations.\n   383→\n   384→    Only admins can create/update/delete designations.\n   385→    \"\"\"\n   386→\n   387→    serializer_class = AICertsInstructorDesignationSerializer\n   388→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n   389→    queryset = AICertsInstructorDesignation.objects.all()\n   390→\n   391→    def perform_create(self, serializer):\n   392→        \"\"\"Record who made the designation\"\"\"\n   393→        serializer.save(designated_by=self.request.user)\n   394→\n   395→\n   396→class AICertsSyncLogViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n   397→    \"\"\"\n   398→    API endpoint for viewing sync logs (read-only).\n   399→\n   400→    Admins can see all logs, users can see their own.\n   401→    \"\"\"\n   402→\n   403→    serializer_class = AICertsSyncLogSerializer\n   404→    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n   405→\n   406→    def get_queryset(self):\n   407→        \"\"\"Filter logs based on user permissions\"\"\"\n   408→        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n   409→            return AICertsSyncLog.objects.all()\n   410→        return AICertsSyncLog.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n   411→\n   412→    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'], url_path='stats')\n   413→    def get_stats(self, request):\n   414→        \"\"\"\n   415→        Get sync statistics.\n   416→\n   417→        Returns counts by operation type and status.\n   418→        \"\"\"\n   419→        from django.db.models import Count\n   420→\n   421→        stats = AICertsSyncLog.objects.values('operation_type', 'status').annotate(\n   422→            count=Count('id')\n   423→        ).order_by('operation_type', 'status')\n   424→\n   425→        return Response({\n   426→            'stats': list(stats)\n   427→        })\n   428→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"uuid":"b6933b38-00cd-4ec0-a4e2-3435a6e44f16","timestamp":"2026-03-01T11:14:45.200Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/views.py","content":"# apps/aicerts_integration/views.py\n\"\"\"\nViews for AICERTs Partnership Integration\n\nHandles:\n- Course enrollment with dual sync (Hosi + AICERTs)\n- SSO redirect generation for course access\n- Instructor validation for course assignments\n- Enrollment status tracking\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom rest_framework import viewsets, status, permissions\nfrom rest_framework.decorators import action\nfrom rest_framework.response import Response\nfrom rest_framework.views import APIView\nfrom django.shortcuts import redirect, get_object_or_404\nfrom django.http import JsonResponse\nfrom django.utils import timezone\nfrom django.db import transaction\nfrom django.conf import settings\nimport logging\nimport secrets\nfrom datetime import timedelta\n\nfrom .models import (\n    AICertsEnrollment,\n    AICertsInstructorDesignation,\n    AICertsSyncLog,\n    AICertsSSOSession\n)\nfrom .services import (\n    SSOService,\n    EnrollmentSyncService,\n    InstructorValidationService,\n    AICERTsAPIError,\n    AICERTsEnrollmentError\n)\nfrom .serializers import (\n    AICertsEnrollmentSerializer,\n    AICertsInstructorDesignationSerializer,\n    AICertsSyncLogSerializer,\n    EnrollUserSerializer,\n    GenerateSSOSerializer\n)\nfrom apps.aicerts_courses.models import AiCertsCourse\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass AICertsEnrollmentViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint for AICERTs course enrollments.\n\n    Features:\n    - List user's enrollments\n    - Create new enrollment (with auto-sync to AICERTs)\n    - Check enrollment status\n    - Retry failed enrollments\n    \"\"\"\n\n    serializer_class = AICertsEnrollmentSerializer\n    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n\n    def get_queryset(self):\n        \"\"\"Users can only see their own enrollments\"\"\"\n        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n            return AICertsEnrollment.objects.all()\n        return AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n\n    @action(detail=False, methods=['post'], url_path='enroll')\n    def enroll_user(self, request):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enroll authenticated user in an AICERTs course.\n\n        Request body:\n        {\n            \"course_id\": 123\n        }\n\n        Response:\n        {\n            \"success\": true,\n            \"enrollment_id\": 456,\n            \"message\": \"Successfully enrolled in course\",\n            \"sso_url\": \"https://learn.aicerts.io/...\"\n        }\n        \"\"\"\n        serializer = EnrollUserSerializer(data=request.data)\n        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n\n        course_id = serializer.validated_data['course_id']\n        course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n\n        try:\n            # Use enrollment sync service for dual enrollment\n            enrollment, aicerts_result = EnrollmentSyncService.enroll_user_in_course(\n                user=request.user,\n                course=course,\n                create_aicerts_user=settings.AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS\n            )\n\n            # Generate SSO URL for immediate course access\n            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n                email=request.user.email,\n                course_id=course_id\n            )\n\n            # Log successful enrollment\n            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n                operation_type='user_enroll',\n                status='success',\n                user=request.user,\n                course=course,\n                response_data=aicerts_result\n            )\n\n            return Response({\n                'success': True,\n                'enrollment_id': enrollment.id,\n                'message': 'Successfully enrolled in course',\n                'sso_url': sso_url,\n                'already_enrolled': enrollment.aicerts_already_enrolled\n            }, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)\n\n        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Enrollment failed for user {request.user.email} in course {course_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Log failed enrollment\n            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n                operation_type='user_enroll',\n                status='failed',\n                user=request.user,\n                course=course,\n                error_message=str(e)\n            )\n\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'Enrollment failed',\n                'details': str(e)\n            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n\n    @action(detail=True, methods=['post'], url_path='retry-sync')\n    def retry_sync(self, request, pk=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Retry failed enrollment sync with AICERTs.\n\n        Only works for enrollments with status='failed' and < 3 attempts.\n        \"\"\"\n        enrollment = self.get_object()\n\n        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'failed':\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'Can only retry failed enrollments'\n            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n\n        if enrollment.sync_attempts >= 3:\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'Maximum retry attempts exceeded'\n            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n\n        try:\n            # Retry enrollment\n            aicerts_result = SSOService.enroll_user(\n                aicerts_user_id=enrollment.user.aicerts_user_id,\n                course_id=enrollment.course.id,\n                email=enrollment.user.email\n            )\n\n            enrollment.mark_synced()\n\n            return Response({\n                'success': True,\n                'message': 'Enrollment synced successfully'\n            })\n\n        except AICERTsEnrollmentError as e:\n            enrollment.mark_failed(str(e))\n\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'Retry failed',\n                'details': str(e)\n            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n\n    @action(detail=True, methods=['get'], url_path='sso-url')\n    def get_sso_url(self, request, pk=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSO URL for this specific enrollment.\n        \"\"\"\n        enrollment = self.get_object()\n\n        if enrollment.aicerts_enrollment_status != 'enrolled':\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'User not enrolled in this course'\n            }, status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN)\n\n        try:\n            # Use external_id (AICERTS/Moodle course ID), not the Django PK\n            aicerts_course_id = enrollment.course.external_id or enrollment.course.id\n            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n                email=enrollment.user.email,\n                course_id=aicerts_course_id\n            )\n            return Response({\n                'success': True,\n                'sso_url': sso_url\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            return Response({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'Failed to generate SSO URL',\n                'details': str(e)\n            }, status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n\n\nclass SSORedirectView(APIView):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate SSO URL and redirect user to AICERTs LMS for course access.\n\n    GET /api/aicerts/sso/redirect/?course_id=123\n\n    Redirects to AICERTs LMS with authenticated session.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n\n    def get(self, request):\n        \"\"\"Generate SSO URL and redirect\"\"\"\n        serializer = GenerateSSOSerializer(data=request.query_params)\n        serializer.is_valid(raise_exception=True)\n\n        course_id = serializer.validated_data.get('course_id')\n        user_id = serializer.validated_data.get('user_id')\n        \n        # Staff can generate SSO for other users (for support/testing)\n        if user_id and request.user.is_staff:\n            target_user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n        else:\n            target_user = request.user\n\n        # Check if user is synced with AICERTs\n        if not target_user.is_synced_with_aicerts():\n            return JsonResponse({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'User account not synced with AICERTs',\n                'message': f'User {target_user.email} not synced with AICERTs'\n            }, status=400)\n\n        # Check if user is enrolled (if course_id provided)\n        if course_id:\n            enrollment = AICertsEnrollment.objects.filter(\n                user=target_user,\n                course_id=course_id,\n                aicerts_enrollment_status='enrolled'\n            ).first()\n\n            if not enrollment:\n                return JsonResponse({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': 'Not enrolled in this course',\n                    'message': 'Please enroll in the course first'\n                }, status=403)\n\n        try:\n            # Generate SSO URL\n            sso_url = SSOService.generate_sso_url(\n                email=target_user.email,\n                course_id=course_id\n            )\n\n            # Create SSO session record\n            course = None\n            if course_id:\n                course = AiCertsCourse.objects.filter(id=course_id).first()\n\n            sso_session = AICertsSSOSession.objects.create(\n                user=target_user,\n                course=course,\n                sso_url=sso_url[:500],  # Truncate for DB storage\n                session_token=secrets.token_urlsafe(32),\n                ip_address=request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR'),\n                user_agent=request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT', '')[:500],\n                expires_at=timezone.now() + timedelta(hours=1)\n            )\n\n            # Log SSO generation\n            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n                operation_type='user_auth',\n                status='success',\n                user=target_user,\n                course=course,\n                response_data={'session_token': sso_session.session_token, 'impersonated_by': request.user.id if user_id else None}\n            )\n\n            # Redirect to AICERTs\n            return redirect(sso_url)\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"SSO generation failed for user {target_user.email}: {e}\")\n\n            AICertsSyncLog.objects.create(\n                operation_type='user_auth',\n                status='failed',\n                user=target_user,\n                error_message=str(e)\n            )\n\n            return JsonResponse({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'SSO generation failed',\n                'details': str(e)\n            }, status=500)\n\n\nclass InstructorValidationView(APIView):\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate instructor eligibility for AICERTs courses.\n\n    GET /api/aicerts/instructor/validate/?user_id=123&course_id=456\n\n    Returns:\n    {\n        \"is_aicerts_instructor\": true,\n        \"can_instruct_course\": true,\n        \"designated_courses\": [...]\n    }\n    \"\"\"\n\n    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n\n    def get(self, request):\n        \"\"\"Check if user can instruct specific course\"\"\"\n        user_id = request.query_params.get('user_id')\n        course_id = request.query_params.get('course_id')\n\n        if not user_id:\n            return Response({\n                'error': 'user_id required'\n            }, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)\n\n        from apps.users.models import User\n        user = get_object_or_404(User, id=user_id)\n\n        response_data = {\n            'user_id': user.id,\n            'email': user.email,\n            'is_aicerts_instructor': user.is_aicerts_instructor,\n            'can_instruct_course': False,\n            'designated_courses': []\n        }\n\n        if user.is_aicerts_instructor:\n            # Get designated courses\n            designated_courses = InstructorValidationService.get_instructor_courses(user)\n            response_data['designated_courses'] = [\n                {\n                    'id': course.id,\n                    'title': course.title,\n                    'shortname': course.shortname\n                }\n                for course in designated_courses\n            ]\n\n            # Check specific course if provided\n            if course_id:\n                course = get_object_or_404(AiCertsCourse, id=course_id)\n                response_data['can_instruct_course'] = InstructorValidationService.can_instruct_course(user, course)\n                response_data['course'] = {\n                    'id': course.id,\n                    'title': course.title\n                }\n\n        return Response(response_data)\n\n\nclass AICertsInstructorDesignationViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint for managing instructor designations.\n\n    Only admins can create/update/delete designations.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    serializer_class = AICertsInstructorDesignationSerializer\n    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAdminUser]\n    queryset = AICertsInstructorDesignation.objects.all()\n\n    def perform_create(self, serializer):\n        \"\"\"Record who made the designation\"\"\"\n        serializer.save(designated_by=self.request.user)\n\n\nclass AICertsSyncLogViewSet(viewsets.ReadOnlyModelViewSet):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint for viewing sync logs (read-only).\n\n    Admins can see all logs, users can see their own.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    serializer_class = AICertsSyncLogSerializer\n    permission_classes = [permissions.IsAuthenticated]\n\n    def get_queryset(self):\n        \"\"\"Filter logs based on user permissions\"\"\"\n        if self.request.user.is_staff:\n            return AICertsSyncLog.objects.all()\n        return AICertsSyncLog.objects.filter(user=self.request.user)\n\n    @action(detail=False, methods=['get'], url_path='stats')\n    def get_stats(self, request):\n        \"\"\"\n        Get sync statistics.\n\n        Returns counts by operation type and status.\n        \"\"\"\n        from django.db.models import Count\n\n        stats = AICertsSyncLog.objects.values('operation_type', 'status').annotate(\n            count=Count('id')\n        ).order_by('operation_type', 'status')\n\n        return Response({\n            'stats': list(stats)\n        })\n","numLines":428,"startLine":1,"totalLines":428}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"648dbc10-763a-4ce2-8f41-4385bf318030"}

'Unknown Course',\n      courseShortname: json['course_shortname'] as String?,\n      status: json['aicerts_enrollment_status'] as String? ?? 'pending',\n      enrolledAt: DateTime.parse(json['enrolled_at'] as String),\n      progress: (json['progress_percentage'] as num?)?.toDouble() ?? 0.0,\n      lastAccessedAt: json['last_accessed_at'] != null\n          ? DateTime.parse(json['last_accessed_at'] as String)\n          : null,\n      completedAt: json['completed_at'] != null\n          ? DateTime.parse(json['completed_at'] as String)\n          : null,\n    );\n  }\n\n  bool get isEnrolled => status == 'enrolled';\n}\n","numLines":46,"startLine":1,"totalLines":46}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e6d89488-f359-4166-abb7-07e5174efe05"}

{"parentUuid":"aa7c5b17-12f4-459f-a25d-3e53f0abee44","isSidechain":false,"userType":"external","cwd":"/home/tk/lms-prod","sessionId":"ed94c593-6e47-4101-9053-fd5ae61d742a","version":"2.1.62","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"valiant-spinning-sky","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.\n\nAnalysis:\nLet me chronologically analyze the entire conversation to create a comprehensive summary.\n\n**Message 1: Richard Masukume login issue**\n- User reported that student Richard Masukume cannot sign in to the student portal\n- Error: \"Invalid email or password\"\n- User said Richard is registered for an AICERTS course\n\n**Investigation:**\n- Found the `users` table had 0 rows - no users in the database\n- Found `auth_service.dart` has hardcoded test credentials including `richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za` with password `password123`\n- Backend uses JWT via `rest_framework_simplejwt`'s `TokenObtainPairView`\n- The frontend sends email as the `username` field but Django's `USERNAME_FIELD` is `username`\n- Found a bug in `aicerts_integration/signals.py`: `auto_create_aicerts_user` was using `timezone.now()` for `aicerts_synced_at` which is a `numeric(20,4)` field\n- `AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS = True` in the container environment\n\n**Actions taken:**\n1. Fixed `signals.py` - changed `timezone.now()` to `time.time()` for `aicerts_synced_at`\n2. Created `apps/users/backends.py` - `EmailOrUsernameBackend` class\n3. Added `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS` to `settings.py`\n4. Created Richard's user account in the DB\n5. Found Richard already exists on AICERTS with user ID 142005\n6. Updated Richard's `aicerts_user_id` to 142005\n\n**Message 2: Console errors - fix the problems**\n- User shared console logs showing:\n  1. `/api/v1/student-portal/content-types/` returning 500\n  2. Login failing with password `TestStudent2026!` (not `password123`)\n\n**Investigation and fixes:**\n1. **500 error**: `ContentType.objects.get(model=model_name)` raised `MultipleObjectsReturned` because `aicertscourse` exists in both `aicerts_courses` and `industry_based_training` apps, and `course` exists in both `courses` and `learnerships`\n   - Fixed: Added `app_label` to each lookup in `views.py`\n\n2. **Password mismatch**: Richard's password was `password123` but he was using `TestStudent2026!`\n   - Shell escaping issue: `!` in bash double-quoted `-c \"...\"` triggers history expansion, stored as `\\!` (17 chars instead of 16)\n   - Fixed: Used printf with `\\x21` hex escape to set the correct password\n   - Also added `EmailOrUsernameBackend` which was needed for email-based auth\n\n**Files modified:**\n- `/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py` - fixed `time.time()` vs `timezone.now()`\n- `/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/users/backends.py` - created new file\n- `/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/lms_project/settings.py` - added `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`\n- `/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/learner_portal/views.py` - fixed `get_content_types` to use `app_label`\n\n**Message 3: Course must appear in student portal**\n- User wants Richard's enrolled AICERTS course to appear in the student portal\n- \"My Courses\" and \"My Enrolled Courses\" sections must show the course\n- Quick stats must be small horizontally aligned cards\n- Richard must be able to click a link in the student portal to access his course on AICERTS LMS\n\n**Investigation done so far:**\n- Explored student portal structure extensively\n- Read `student_portal_page.dart` (832 lines) fully\n- Read `aicerts_enrollment.dart` model\n- Read `aicerts_integration/views.py` (backend)\n- The dashboard currently has large vertical stat cards (not horizontal)\n- The `_buildMyCoursesView()` properly fetches from `/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/`\n- The `_launchCourse()` method gets SSO URL and opens it\n- No `AICertsEnrollment` record exists in the DB for Richard\n- The `_buildStatCard()` is a large vertical card - needs to become small horizontal\n\n**Pending work:** \n1. Create Richard's `AICertsEnrollment` record in the DB (linking him to his specific AICERTS course)\n2. Fix the quick stats to be small horizontal cards\n3. Fix the \"Recent Course Activity\" section to show real enrollment data from `_enrollments`\n4. Update the enrolled count stat to use real `_enrollments.length`\n5. Build and deploy the frontend changes\n\nThe work was cut off right at the point of reading `student_portal_page.dart` and understanding what needs to change - specifically the stat cards need to be horizontal, and Richard needs an enrollment record.\n\nThe user's most recent request was about making the AICERTS course appear in the student portal with:\n- \"My Courses\" and \"My Enrolled Courses\" sections\n- Quick stats as small horizontal cards\n- Click through to AICERTS LMS via SSO\n\nThe immediate next step is:\n1. Create Richard's enrollment record in the DB\n2. Modify the stat cards to be horizontal\n3. Update dashboard to show real enrollment data\n4. Build and deploy\n\nSummary:\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n\n**Request 1 (Initial):** Student Richard Masukume (`richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za`) cannot sign in to the student portal. Getting \"Invalid email or password\" error. He is registered for an AICERTS course and should be able to navigate to it in the student portal.\n\n**Request 2 (Console errors):** Fix multiple errors showing in the browser console:\n- `/api/v1/student-portal/content-types/` returning HTTP 500\n- Login failing with password `TestStudent2026!` (Richard was using this, not `password123`)\n\n**Request 3 (Current/Most Recent):** The AICERTS course Richard is enrolled in must appear in the student portal. Specifically:\n- The course must appear in \"My Courses\" and display in \"My Enrolled Courses\"\n- Quick stats must be **small horizontally aligned cards** (not large vertical ones)\n- Richard must be able to click a link inside the Student Portal to access his course on the AICERTS LMS (via SSO)\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n- Django JWT authentication via `rest_framework_simplejwt` (`TokenObtainPairView`)\n- Django `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS` — custom `EmailOrUsernameBackend` needed because frontend sends email in `username` field\n- Django `ContentType` framework — `MultipleObjectsReturned` when same model name exists in multiple apps\n- AICERTS SSO integration — HMAC-signed URLs to `learn.aicerts.io` via `SSOService.generate_sso_url()`\n- `AICertsEnrollment` model — links LMS users to AICERTS courses, tracks sync status\n- Flutter Web student portal — `StudentPortalPage` with 4 tabs: Dashboard, My Courses, Wishlist, Progress\n- Bash shell `!` history expansion bug — `!` in `-c \"...\"` gets escaped to `\\!`, corrupting password storage\n- Docker `docker cp` + `docker restart` pattern for deploying code changes (no volume mount)\n- `aicerts_synced_at` field is `DecimalField` (epoch timestamp), not `DateTimeField`\n- `AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS = True` in production container env\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/signals.py`**\n  - Bug: `auto_create_aicerts_user` used `timezone.now()` for `aicerts_synced_at` (a `numeric(20,4)` epoch field) → caused `ValidationError` on user creation\n  - Fix: Added `import time` and changed to `aicerts_synced_at=time.time()`\n  - Also fixed: removed `from django.utils import timezone` dependency for this field\n  ```python\n  import time\n  # ...\n  User.objects.filter(pk=instance.pk).update(\n      aicerts_user_id=aicerts_user_id,\n      aicerts_synced_at=time.time()  # was: timezone.now()\n  )\n  ```\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/users/backends.py`** *(NEW FILE)*\n  - Created custom authentication backend so email can be used as username for JWT login\n  - Frontend always sends email address in `username` field; Django's default `ModelBackend` only matches `username` field\n  ```python\n  from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model\n  from django.contrib.auth.backends import ModelBackend\n\n  class EmailOrUsernameBackend(ModelBackend):\n      def authenticate(self, request, username=None, password=None, **kwargs):\n          User = get_user_model()\n          if username is None:\n              return None\n          try:\n              user = User.objects.get(email__iexact=username)\n          except User.DoesNotExist:\n              try:\n                  user = User.objects.get(username__iexact=username)\n              except User.DoesNotExist:\n                  return None\n              except Exception:\n                  return None\n          except Exception:\n              return None\n          if user.check_password(password) and self.user_can_authenticate(user):\n              return user\n          return None\n  ```\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/lms_project/settings.py`**\n  - Added `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS` setting after `AUTH_USER_MODEL`\n  ```python\n  AUTH_USER_MODEL = 'users.User'\n  AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS = [\n      'apps.users.backends.EmailOrUsernameBackend',\n      'django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend',\n  ]\n  ```\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/learner_portal/views.py`**\n  - Fixed `get_content_types` function (lines ~631-647): `ContentType.objects.get(model=model_name)` raised `MultipleObjectsReturned` because `aicertscourse` exists in both `aicerts_courses` and `industry_based_training` apps, and `course` exists in both `courses` and `learnerships`\n  - Fix: switched to list of `(model_name, app_label)` tuples and used `get(app_label=app_label, model=model_name)`\n  ```python\n  model_lookup = [\n      ('masterclass',          'masterclasses'),\n      ('learnershipprogramme', 'learnerships'),\n      ('aicertscourse',        'aicerts_courses'),\n      ('offering',             'industry_based_training'),\n      ('course',               'courses'),\n  ]\n  content_types = {}\n  for model_name, app_label in model_lookup:\n      try:\n          ct = ContentType.objects.get(app_label=app_label, model=model_name)\n          content_types[model_name] = ct.id\n      except ContentType.DoesNotExist:\n          content_types[model_name] = None\n  return Response(content_types)\n  ```\n  - Result: endpoint now returns `{\"masterclass\":75,\"learnershipprogramme\":13,\"aicertscourse\":7,\"offering\":74,\"course\":9}`\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/core/services/auth_service.dart`**\n  - Read-only; contains hardcoded `_testCredentials` map including `richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za` with password `password123`\n  - Login flow: tries backend JWT first, falls back to hardcoded credentials on `DioException`\n  - The frontend always sends `'username': trimmedEmail` (email address) to the login endpoint\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/core/api/api_client.dart`**\n  - Read-only; Dio-based HTTP client; re-throws `DioException` on 4xx/5xx\n  - Key AICERTS methods: `getAICertsEnrollments()` → `GET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/`, `getAICertsSSOUrl(id)` → `GET /api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/{id}/sso-url/`\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/backend/apps/aicerts_integration/views.py`**\n  - Read-only; `AICertsEnrollmentViewSet.get_sso_url()` uses `course.external_id` (the AICERTS/Moodle course ID) to generate SSO URL\n  - `AICertsEnrollmentViewSet.get_queryset()` filters by `user=request.user`\n  - SSO URL endpoint at `/api/v1/aicerts/enrollments/{id}/sso-url/`\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/data/models/aicerts_enrollment.dart`**\n  - Read-only; model class mapping JSON from backend\n  - Key fields: `id`, `courseId` (from `json['course']`), `courseTitle` (from `json['course_title']`), `status` (from `json['aicerts_enrollment_status']`), `enrolledAt`, `progress`\n  - `isEnrolled` getter: `status == 'enrolled'`\n\n- **`/home/tk/lms-prod/frontend/lib/src/presentation/pages/student_portal/student_portal_page.dart`**\n  - Read fully (832 lines); main student portal page\n  - `_buildDashboardView()`: Welcome section + 3 stat cards in `Wrap` layout (currently large vertical cards) + hardcoded \"Recent Course Activity\"\n  - `_buildStatCard()`: Large vertical card with `padding: 24`, `Column` layout with icon on top, big headline value, label below — **needs to become small horizontal**\n  - `_buildMyCoursesView()`: Shows `_enrollments` list; if empty shows \"Your learning materials will appear here\"; if populated shows `ListView` of `_buildEnrollmentCard()`\n  - `_buildEnrollmentCard()`: Shows course icon, title, status badge, progress bar, \"Launch Course\" button that calls `_launchCourse()`\n  - `_launchCourse()`: Gets SSO URL via `ApiClient.getAICertsSSOUrl(enrollment.id)` and opens with `launchUrl(uri, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication)`\n  - Stats values are hardcoded (`'4'`, `'1'`, `'12.5h'`) — should use real `_enrollments.length`\n  - Recent activity section shows 2 hardcoded cards — should show real enrollment data\n\n4. Errors and fixes:\n\n- **`ValueError: Cannot assign \"'South Africa'\": \"User.country\" must be a \"Country\" instance`**\n  - Occurred when running `create_test_users` management command\n  - The command passed string `'South Africa'` to `country` field which expects a FK\n  - Fix: Created Richard's account directly via Django shell without country/city fields\n\n- **`django.core.exceptions.ValidationError: ['\"2026-03-01 09:46:44...\" value must be a decimal number']`**\n  - Occurred during user creation via Django shell\n  - Root cause: `auto_create_aicerts_user` signal (in `signals.py`) tried to set `aicerts_synced_at=timezone.now()` but the field is `DecimalField` (epoch timestamp)\n  - Fix: Added `import time`, changed to `aicerts_synced_at=time.time()`\n\n- **`django.contrib.contenttypes.models.ContentType.MultipleObjectsReturned: get() returned more than one ContentType -- it returned 2!`**\n  - `/api/v1/student-portal/content-types/` returned HTTP 500\n  - Root cause: `aicertscourse` model exists in both `aicerts_courses` and `industry_based_training` apps; `course` in both `courses` and `learnerships`\n  - Fix: Specified `app_label` explicitly in each `ContentType.objects.get()` call\n\n- **Login returning 401 despite correct password in shell**\n  - Root cause 1: `USERNAME_FIELD` is `username` but frontend sends email — no email auth backend existed\n  - Fix: Created `EmailOrUsernameBackend` and added to `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`\n  - Root cause 2: Bash `!` history expansion in `docker exec ... -c \"...\"` turned `TestStudent2026!` into `TestStudent2026\\!` (17 chars), making stored hash wrong\n  - Discovery method: Logged password bytes, found `[..., 92, 33]` (92=backslash, 33=`!`)\n  - Fix: Used `printf '%s\\n' \"...set_password('TestStudent2026\\x21')...\"` with hex escape `\\x21` for `!`\n  - Also confirmed: `@` character worked fine; `!` consistently failed via HTTP when set via shell `-c`\n\n- **`AICERTS_AUTO_CREATE_USERS = True` causing signal to fire on user creation**\n  - When creating Richard via shell with `email_verified_at=timezone.now()`, the signal called AICERTS API and got back `{'status': 'success', 'message': 'User already exists', 'id': 142005}` (Richard already existed on AICERTS), then tried to update `aicerts_synced_at` with datetime → decimal error\n  - Fix: The `signals.py` fix (using `time.time()`) resolved this\n\n5. Problem Solving:\n\n**Solved:**\n- Richard's LMS user account created (ID=1, email `richard.masukume@hosiacademy.co.za`, role_id=3)\n- Richard's AICERTS user ID linked (142005, already existed on AICERTS)\n- Email-based JWT authentication working (`POST /api/v1/auth/login/` with email returns tokens)\n- Richard's password correctly set to `TestStudent2026!` (using hex escape)\n- `/api/v1/student-portal/content-types/` returning 200 with correct content type IDs\n- Backend `docker restart` strategy confirmed: `docker cp` files then `docker restart` preserves changes since container has no volume mount\n\n**Ongoing:**\n- Richard has no `AICertsEnrollment` record in the `aicerts_enrollments` table — the specific AICERTS course he's enrolled in is unknown (user was asked to check AICERTS admin panel)\n- The student portal's \"My Courses\" tab currently shows empty state for Richard because no enrollment record exists\n- The dashboard stat cards are large vertical layout, need to be small horizontal cards\n- The \"Recent Course Activity\" section shows hardcoded data, not real enrollments\n\n6. All user messages:\n- \"Student called Richard Masukume must have access to his AICERTS course that he is registered for and he is supposed to be able to navigate to that course in the student portal. Currently he cannot sign in. An error:'Invalid email or password' error is being displayed instead\"\n- \"Fix the problems here: [large console log paste showing]: 500 error on `/api/v1/student-portal/content-types/`, login failing with `TestStudent2026!` password, 401 response. The sign in test case is not working. Fix it.\"\n- \"The course that the student is enrolled for must appear in the student portal allowing the student to click and navigate to the AICERTS LMS for that course. The AICERTS course must be availed in 'My Courses' and displayed in 'My Enrolled Courses'. The quick stats must be small horizontally aligned cards. Richard must be able to access the course by clicking inside the Student portal link to his course\"\n\n7. Pending Tasks:\n- **Create `AICertsEnrollment` record** for Richard in the LMS database linking him to his specific AICERTS course (need to know which course — user to confirm from AICERTS admin panel; Richard's AICERTS user ID is 142005)\n- **Refactor quick stats** in `_buildDashboardView()` in `student_portal_page.dart`: change from large vertical `Column`-based cards (24px padding, big headline) to **small horizontally-aligned cards** using `Row` layout\n- **Update stat values** to use real data: \"Enrolled Courses\" should show `_enrollments.length` instead of hardcoded `'4'`\n- **Update \"Recent Course Activity\"** section to show real AICERTS enrollments from `_enrollments` list instead of two hardcoded placeholder cards\n- **Build and deploy frontend**: `flutter build web --dart-define=API_BASE_URL=https://hosiacademy.africa --release --pwa-strategy=none` then `docker cp build/web/. lms-prod-frontend-1:/usr/share/nginx/html/`\n\n8. _userName\n    29→  int _unreadMessages = 2; // Restore _unreadMessages\n    30→\n    31→  // Pages for the portal\n    32→  late final List<Widget> _pages;\n    33→\n    34→  // Real enrollment data\n    35→  List<AICertsEnrollment> _enrollments = [];\n    36→  bool _isLoadingEnrollments = false;\n    37→\n    38→  @override\n    39→  void initState() {\n    40→    super.initState();\n WishlistPage(embedMode: true),\n    47→      _buildProgressView(),\n    48→    ];\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  Future<void> _fetchEnrollments() async {\n    52→    if (!mounted) return;\n    53→    setState(() => _isLoadingEnrollments = true);\n    54→    try {\n    55→      final data = await ApiClient.getAICertsEnrollments();\n    56→      if (!mounted) return;\n    57→      setState(() {\n with new data\n    63→        _pages[1] = _buildMyCoursesView();\n    64→      });\n    65→    } catch (e) {\n    66→      debugPrint('Error fetching enrollments: $e');\n    67→      if (!mounted) return;\n    68→      setState(() => _isLoadingEnrollments = false);\n    69→    }\n    70→  }\n    71→\n    72→  Future<void> _launchCourse(AICertsEnrollment enrollment) async {\n    73→    try {\n
